passage: The Department of Veterans Affairs requires the contractor to employ a waterfall methodology for the development of the Veterans Benefits Management System Enhancement. All work shall proceed through sequential phases including requirements analysis, system design, implementation, testing, deployment, and maintenance. Each phase must be fully completed and formally accepted by the Contracting Officer's Representative prior to initiation of the subsequent phase. The contractor shall maintain a fixed requirements baseline established during the requirements phase, which shall not be altered without formal change control board approval and contract modification. Deliverables associated with each phase gate must be submitted, reviewed, and approved before any downstream development activities commence. The contractor shall provide a detailed Waterfall Project Plan within 30 days of contract award, identifying all phase entry and exit criteria, milestone dates, and associated deliverables. Monthly status reports shall track progress against the approved baseline schedule and document any variances exceeding five percent of planned effort.

The Department of Homeland Security, Office of the Chief Information Officer, solicits proposals for the modernization of the Personnel Security Adjudication Platform. The Government mandates use of a traditional SDLC approach consistent with DHS policy directives governing enterprise system development. The contractor shall execute the project through clearly delineated sequential phases: Concept of Operations, Requirements Definition, Preliminary Design, Critical Design, Code and Unit Test, Integration and Test, System Acceptance Test, and Operational Deployment. A formal phase gate review shall be conducted at the conclusion of each phase, with Government approval required before work proceeds. Requirements established at the conclusion of the Requirements Definition phase shall constitute the fixed requirements baseline and shall be placed under configuration management control. No development work shall begin prior to Government approval of the System Requirements Specification document.

The General Services Administration, Public Buildings Service, hereby solicits offers for development of the Facilities Asset Lifecycle Tracking System. In accordance with GSA Order CIO 2100.1, the contractor shall utilize standard software lifecycle development practices as the governing development framework for this engagement. The project shall be organized into five sequential phases: Requirements Gathering, Architecture and Design, Development, Testing and Quality Assurance, and Deployment and Transition. Phase-to-phase progression is contingent upon successful completion of formal Government review and written authorization to proceed. The contractor is expressly prohibited from initiating coding activities prior to approval of the Functional Requirements Document and System Architecture Document. A fixed requirements baseline shall be established no later than 60 calendar days after contract award. Subsequent changes to baselined requirements must be processed through the formal Engineering Change Proposal process and approved in writing by the Contracting Officer.

The Department of Defense, Defense Logistics Agency, requires development of the Materiel Tracking and Accountability System under a waterfall development model consistent with MIL-STD-498 and DoD 5000.02 acquisition guidelines. The contractor shall conduct all software development activities in strict sequential phases including Software Requirements Analysis, Preliminary Design, Detailed Design, Coding and Unit Testing, Computer Software Component Integration and Testing, Computer Software Configuration Item Testing, and System Integration and Testing. The V-model verification and validation framework shall be applied throughout, with each left-side development phase paired with a corresponding right-side testing activity. Phase gate reviews, including the System Requirements Review, Preliminary Design Review, and Critical Design Review, shall be conducted at prescribed milestones. Entrance and exit criteria for each phase gate are defined in Attachment J-3. No phase may commence without written Government approval documenting satisfaction of all exit criteria from the preceding phase.

The Social Security Administration, Office of Systems, issues this Request for Proposal for the Disability Determination Services Workflow Automation Project. The contractor shall manage all development activities in accordance with SSA's Enterprise Systems Development Methodology, which prescribes a waterfall approach to system delivery. Project execution shall follow sequential phases as follows: Business Requirements Definition, Logical Design, Physical Design, Construction, System Integration Testing, User Acceptance Testing, and Production Deployment. Each phase produces mandatory deliverables that must receive formal written acceptance from the SSA Project Manager before work on the subsequent phase is authorized to begin. The contractor shall establish and maintain a fixed requirements baseline upon Government acceptance of the Business Requirements Specification. All proposed modifications to baselined requirements shall be submitted via the SSA Change Request process and are subject to impact assessment, Configuration Control Board review, and written approval prior to incorporation.

The Department of Transportation, Federal Aviation Administration, solicits technical and price proposals for development of the Aeronautical Information Management Replacement System. Given the safety-critical nature of the system, the FAA mandates compliance with DO-178C and requires the use of traditional SDLC sequential phases throughout the program lifecycle. The contractor shall complete and obtain Government approval of all systems engineering artifacts associated with each development phase before proceeding to subsequent phases. The development sequence shall include: System Requirements Review, Software Requirements Review, Preliminary Design Review, Critical Design Review, Software Build and Integration, Software Qualification Testing, System Acceptance Testing, and Operational Readiness Review. A fixed requirements baseline shall be established following Government approval of the System and Software Requirements Specifications. The contractor shall employ rigorous phase gate controls, and no phase transition shall occur without documented Government authorization. Traceability matrices shall be maintained from requirements through test.

The Department of Energy, National Nuclear Security Administration, requires that the contractor employ a waterfall methodology for the development of the Nuclear Material Accountability and Control Information System. This requirement reflects NNSA policy mandating structured, auditable development processes for systems handling Safeguards and Security information. The contractor shall execute all software development under clearly defined sequential phases: Requirements Definition, Functional Design, Detailed Design, Software Construction, Software Testing, and System Deployment. Phase gate reviews shall be scheduled and completed at the conclusion of each phase, with a formal Government Decision Authority review required before transition to the next phase. All requirements identified during the Requirements Definition phase shall be placed under configuration control as the fixed requirements baseline. Changes to this baseline require submission of a formal Request for Change, independent cost and schedule impact assessment, and written approval from the NNSA Contracting Officer's Representative and Configuration Control Board.

The Department of Justice, Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives, solicits proposals for the Federal Firearms Licensee Inspection Management System. The contractor shall develop the system in accordance with ATF's Information Systems Security and Development Policy, which requires use of a standard software lifecycle development approach for all major applications. The development effort shall be organized into the following sequential phases: Initiation and Planning, Requirements Analysis, System Design, Development and Unit Testing, Integration Testing, User Acceptance Testing, Security Assessment and Authorization, and Production Deployment. Each phase shall conclude with a formal milestone review and production of Government-accepted phase deliverables before the subsequent phase is initiated. A fixed requirements baseline shall be documented and approved in writing no later than 45 days following contract award. The contractor shall not begin any system design activities until the Requirements Traceability Matrix and System Requirements Specification have been formally approved by the Government.

The Department of Agriculture, Farm Service Agency, hereby issues a solicitation for development of the Commodity Price Support Calculation Engine. FSA requires the contractor to employ a waterfall development lifecycle consistent with USDA's Departmental Regulation 3300-001 governing enterprise application development. The contractor shall organize all development activities into the following sequential phases: Requirements Elicitation and Validation, System Architecture and Design, Database Design, Application Development, Unit and Integration Testing, System Testing, User Acceptance Testing, and Production Implementation. Formal phase gate reviews shall be conducted at the completion of each phase, and the Government's written approval to proceed is a prerequisite for initiation of any subsequent phase. A fixed requirements baseline shall be established upon Government acceptance of the Approved System Requirements Document. Modifications to the approved baseline are subject to the FSA Change Management process, require documented business justification, and must receive written Contracting Officer authorization before implementation.

The Department of the Treasury, Internal Revenue Service, solicits proposals for the Tax Exempt Organization Compliance Tracking System under IRS Publication 4977 enterprise development standards. The contractor shall execute all development activities using a waterfall methodology as prescribed by IRS IT Policy IRM 2.7.1, which governs the structured delivery of systems processing taxpayer information. The contractor shall adhere to the following sequential phases: Business and Functional Requirements, Technical Architecture, Detailed Design, Application Construction, Component Integration Testing, Performance and Security Testing, User Acceptance Testing, and Deployment to Production. Phase gate reviews at each phase boundary shall include mandatory participation by IRS Information Systems Security personnel, the Project Management Office, and business stakeholders. Written authorization from the IRS Investment Review Board is required before progressing past each phase gate. The fixed requirements baseline, established at the conclusion of the Business and Functional Requirements phase, shall be maintained under formal configuration management control throughout the project lifecycle.

The Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) is seeking a qualified contractor to configure and deploy a commercial off-the-shelf customer relationship management solution to support the Veterans Benefits Administration's case intake and tracking functions. The Government has determined that Salesforce Government Cloud meets the functional requirements identified in the market research phase and represents the most cost-effective approach. The contractor shall be responsible for configuring Salesforce workflows, custom objects, permission sets, and approval processes in alignment with VA business rules. No custom software development is anticipated under this effort. The contractor shall also be responsible for data migration from legacy Access databases, user acceptance testing coordination, and end-user training for approximately 400 VBA personnel across regional offices. The period of performance is a twelve-month base period with two twelve-month option periods. All work shall comply with VA Handbook 6500, FISMA requirements, and FedRAMP authorization standards applicable to the selected commercial solution.

The General Services Administration (GSA) Office of Strategic Communication hereby solicits proposals for the configuration, customization, and deployment of a SharePoint Online-based intranet portal to replace the agency's aging on-premises collaboration environment. The Government intends to leverage the existing Microsoft 365 enterprise licensing agreement and does not require the development of proprietary software. Contractors shall provide SharePoint configuration services including taxonomy design, metadata schema development, site collection architecture, and integration with existing Azure Active Directory identity management. The scope explicitly excludes custom application development. Deliverables include a configured SharePoint environment, governance documentation, administrator training, and a phased migration plan for approximately 12,000 legacy content items. Offerors must demonstrate a minimum of three years of experience deploying SharePoint as a commercial solution within federal government environments and shall provide three relevant past performance references from federal civilian agencies.

The U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA) District Office in Atlanta, Georgia is issuing this Request for Proposals for web presence modernization services. The SBA seeks a contractor to deploy and configure a pre-built platform solution suitable for a public-facing informational website supporting small business resource partners in the Southeast region. The Government has conducted market research and identified WordPress as a viable commercial off-the-shelf solution that satisfies the accessibility, content management, and multilingual support requirements outlined herein. The contractor shall configure and deploy a WordPress-based site consistent with current Section 508 standards and OMB M-23-22 digital experience guidance. Scope of work includes theme configuration, plugin selection and security hardening, content population from existing materials, Google Analytics integration, and a two-year maintenance support arrangement. Custom software development is expressly out of scope. The contractor shall deliver a fully operational site within 90 days of contract award.

The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Science and Technology Directorate is soliciting proposals for the implementation of a cloud-based project portfolio management capability to support research program tracking across the Directorate. Following a formal analysis of alternatives, the Government has selected a commercial solution based on Salesforce Project Management modules as the technical baseline. The primary work under this contract is configuring and deploying the selected commercial off-the-shelf platform to meet DHS-specific reporting, resource allocation, and milestone tracking requirements. The contractor shall configure dashboards, automated notifications, role-based access controls, and integration connectors to existing financial management systems. Offerors shall describe their approach to Salesforce configuration without reliance on custom Apex code development except where configuration-only approaches are technically infeasible and documented as such. The Government anticipates a total level of effort of approximately 2,800 hours across the base year, with options for ongoing platform administration and enhancement.

The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) Office of Education invites qualified vendors to submit proposals for the redesign and deployment of a public educational outreach website. NOAA has determined that a pre-built platform such as Squarespace or an equivalent commercially available website builder satisfies the functional needs of this effort, which is primarily informational in nature and does not require custom application development or backend database integration. The contractor shall configure and deploy the selected commercial platform, ensuring full compliance with NOAA digital branding standards, Section 508 accessibility requirements, and applicable federal web policy. Deliverables include site architecture documentation, configured and populated site with a minimum of 50 content pages, staff training for designated content editors, and a 12-month support retainer for platform administration. Proposals shall identify the specific commercial off-the-shelf platform proposed, justify its selection against the functional requirements in Section C, and provide a fixed-price implementation schedule.

The Department of Education, Office of Elementary and Secondary Education (OESE), is seeking contractor support for the implementation of a grants management tracking and stakeholder communication system. The Government has completed its acquisition planning and determined that a commercial off-the-shelf solution built upon the Salesforce platform provides the optimal balance of cost, functionality, and security posture. The contractor shall configure Salesforce to support grant application intake, reviewer assignment workflows, award notification processes, and recipient reporting tracking. Integration with the Department's existing identity provider using SAML 2.0 federation is required. The contractor is explicitly prohibited from developing custom software components where configuration-based solutions exist within the platform's native capabilities. The Government will provide a licensed Salesforce environment; the contractor is responsible solely for configuration, testing, training, and go-live support. The acquisition is set aside for small businesses under NAICS 541511, though the primary deliverable remains platform configuration rather than software development.

The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Southwestern Division, hereby issues this solicitation for SharePoint-based knowledge management system services. The Division has standardized on Microsoft SharePoint Online as its enterprise collaboration platform under the Army's existing M365 enterprise agreement and requires a contractor to configure and deploy a structured knowledge repository supporting engineering project documentation, lessons learned capture, and cross-district information sharing. The contractor shall design and implement a SharePoint site collection hierarchy, configure content types, managed metadata term stores, search schema, and retention policies consistent with Army Records Management requirements. The Government does not require and will not fund proprietary software development under this task order. The contractor shall provide all configuration through SharePoint's native administrative interfaces and approved Power Platform tools. Training shall be provided for up to 60 knowledge managers across six district offices. Final deliverables include a fully operational configured SharePoint environment and comprehensive administrator and end-user documentation.

The Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS), Office of Communications, is soliciting vendor proposals for the modernization of an internal staff communications hub. CMS has determined through market research that deploying a commercial solution leveraging SharePoint Online and Microsoft Viva Connections meets the functional requirements identified by program stakeholders. The contractor shall configure and deploy these commercial off-the-shelf tools within the existing CMS Microsoft 365 tenant, including designing the information architecture, configuring Viva Connections dashboard cards, establishing permissions aligned to CMS organizational hierarchy, and integrating the hub with existing SharePoint news publishing workflows. Custom development is not within scope; all solutions must be achievable through native platform configuration. The contractor shall conduct structured discovery sessions with CMS communications staff, produce configuration design documentation subject to CMS approval prior to implementation, and deliver a fully operational hub within 120 days of contract award along with training materials for approximately 200 content contributors.

The Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) Individual Assistance Division is seeking a contractor to support the configuration and deployment of a constituent relationship management system to improve disaster survivor communication and follow-up tracking. Based on prior investment and existing agency licensing, FEMA has designated Salesforce as the approved commercial platform for this effort. The contractor shall configure Salesforce Service Cloud to support survivor intake case records, automated follow-up task generation, multi-channel communication logging, and supervisor escalation workflows. The contractor shall not introduce custom-developed code unless a formal deviation request is approved by the FEMA Technical Review Board, and all work shall prioritize declarative configuration approaches within Salesforce. The contractor must hold a valid Salesforce implementation partner agreement and provide certified Salesforce administrators and consultants as key personnel. The base period of performance is twelve months. FEMA anticipates issuing a Request for Task Order Proposals under an existing Blanket Purchase Agreement vehicle.

The Department of the Interior, Bureau of Indian Affairs (BIA), issues this solicitation for website design and content management services for the BIA Office of Trust Services public information portal. The Government intends to deploy a commercial off-the-shelf content management platform, specifically Wix Enterprise or an equivalent commercially available solution, to support public-facing informational content delivery without incurring custom software development costs. The contractor shall configure and deploy the selected commercial platform, develop site templates consistent with USA.gov design standards and BIA branding guidelines, populate initial content provided by the BIA communications team, and establish a content governance workflow for ongoing updates. Proposals must address how the proposed pre-built platform solution achieves compliance with Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act and WCAG 2.1 Level AA standards. The contractor shall provide a minimum of eight hours of training for designated BIA content administrators and shall remain available for platform administration support during the 12-month base period and two option years.

The Department of Veterans Affairs is seeking a Commercial Off-the-Shelf (COTS) solution for enterprise human resources management. Offerors must provide a fully licensed, commercially available software platform that meets the functional requirements outlined in this solicitation. No custom software development will be performed under this contract. The Government requires configuration only to align the solution with VA-specific workflows, user roles, and reporting hierarchies. Vendors who propose proprietary or purpose-built applications developed specifically for this requirement will be considered non-responsive. The selected solution must be a product currently available in the commercial marketplace with an established user base of no fewer than fifty enterprise customers. Implementation services shall be limited to system configuration, data migration, and user training. All modifications to core application functionality are expressly prohibited. The Contractor shall document all configuration settings to ensure repeatability and auditability. Any identified gaps between COTS functionality and agency requirements shall be addressed through process re-engineering on the Government side, not through development of new software features.

The General Services Administration Office of Technology Policy is issuing this Request for Proposal for a cloud-based contract writing and acquisition management platform. This procurement is strictly limited to the acquisition of an existing commercial solution. No software development is authorized under any circumstances. Offerors shall not propose to build, code, or engineer new application modules in response to agency-specific requirements. The Government's intent is to leverage a proven, market-available product and adapt internal business processes to align with the tool's existing capabilities. Configuration of user permissions, workflow routing, document templates, and integration with existing financial systems via standard APIs is permissible and expected. Offerors must demonstrate that the proposed platform has been deployed in at least three federal agency environments. Proposals that include statements of work referencing sprint cycles, agile development backlogs, or custom feature engineering will be evaluated unfavorably and may be deemed non-compliant with this solicitation's scope restrictions.

The Department of Homeland Security, Office of the Chief Information Officer, hereby solicits proposals for an enterprise identity and access management solution. This requirement is for a COTS solution required to meet the agency's zero-trust architecture objectives. The Government will not fund, authorize, or accept delivery of any custom-developed software components. Offerors are advised that the scope of this engagement is configuration only, encompassing identity provider setup, multi-factor authentication policy definition, role-based access control mapping, and directory synchronization. All proposed software must be commercially available and actively maintained by the Offeror as a standard product offering. Licensing terms must reflect commercial market rates, and the product must receive regular security patches and version updates as part of the vendor's standard release cycle. Integration connectors used to link the platform with DHS legacy systems must be pre-built and commercially supported. Engineering of new connectors or middleware specifically for this contract is outside the permissible scope of work.

Pursuant to Federal Acquisition Regulation Part 12, the Department of Education is conducting a commercial item acquisition for a learning management system to support Title I program administration training. This solicitation does not contemplate and will not fund any new software engineering effort. No custom development of application features, modules, dashboards, or reporting tools is included within scope. The awarded Contractor shall be responsible for configuration only, including course catalog setup, learner enrollment workflows, administrator role assignments, and integration with the Department's single sign-on environment using the platform's existing API connectors. The Government acknowledges that the selected COTS platform may not fulfill one hundred percent of desired capabilities and accepts that business processes will be adapted accordingly. Offerors should not propose feature enhancements or product roadmap commitments as part of their technical approach. Evaluation criteria will focus on the extent to which the existing, unmodified platform addresses stated requirements without reliance on future development promises.

The Social Security Administration is seeking a vendor to implement a COTS document management and workflow automation solution within the Office of Disability Determinations. This is a firm-fixed-price acquisition for commercial software licensing, implementation, and ongoing support. The scope of this contract is expressly limited to the deployment and configuration of an existing commercial product. No custom software development will be permitted. Offerors may not propose to write new code, develop new application interfaces, or engineer bespoke processing logic to satisfy SSA-specific business rules. Where the commercial product includes configurable rules engines, form builders, or workflow designers, the Contractor may use those tools to tailor the solution to SSA requirements. Such activities constitute configuration and are within scope. However, any effort requiring software compilation, version branching, or source code modification is strictly prohibited and shall not be included in any proposal. SSA reserves the right to reject any proposal that conflates configuration services with software development activity.

The Department of Transportation Federal Highway Administration hereby requests proposals for an enterprise asset management platform supporting the National Bridge Inspection Program. Consistent with FHWA's technology modernization policy, this acquisition mandates a COTS solution and explicitly prohibits any custom application development. Vendors shall not include development labor categories, software engineering hours, or coding deliverables within their proposed staffing plans. The work under this contract is scoped as configuration only, covering asset taxonomy definition, inspection workflow setup, geospatial layer integration using the platform's native mapping connectors, and report template customization through the product's built-in report designer. The Contractor must demonstrate that all proposed capabilities are available in the current production release of the software and do not depend on future product updates or vendor commitments. Proposals referencing software development lifecycles, code repositories, or testing environments associated with new feature creation will be considered outside the scope of this solicitation and may result in the proposal being found technically unacceptable.

The Department of the Interior Bureau of Indian Affairs is soliciting offers for a grants management information system to replace legacy tracking tools currently in use across twelve regional offices. The Government's acquisition strategy for this requirement is based on the procurement of a commercially available, pre-built platform. No custom software development is authorized under this contract. The BIA recognizes that a COTS solution required approach may necessitate adjustment of internal grant lifecycle processes to conform to the system's standard workflows. This trade-off is acceptable to the Government and has been incorporated into the acquisition planning assumptions. The Contractor's role is limited to system configuration, user acceptance testing support, training delivery, and help desk services during a twelve-month transition period. Any proposal that includes line items for software engineers, developers, architects engaged in coding activity, or application development environments will be evaluated as non-compliant with the stated scope. Questions regarding permissible configuration activities should be submitted through the official solicitation question-and-answer process.

The Department of Justice Office of Justice Programs is issuing this solicitation for a performance measurement and reporting platform to support grant recipient monitoring activities. OJP requires a commercially available product with demonstrated use in federal grant management contexts. This is not a software development contract. No custom development of any application component is within scope, and proposals containing such elements will be rejected. The Government's intent is to configure an existing platform to capture grantee performance data, generate compliance reports, and provide dashboard visualizations using tools native to the commercial product. Configuration-only work is authorized, including form design using the platform's built-in form builder, workflow definition using the platform's process configuration module, and user access management. The Contractor shall provide a configuration workbook documenting all settings applied during implementation. OJP will retain the ability to perform ongoing configuration adjustments using administrative tools provided within the product. No source code access, compiled code deliverables, or software release packages are expected or permitted under this contract.

The Department of Energy Office of Environmental Management hereby solicits proposals for a project controls and earned value management system to support cleanup program oversight at multiple national laboratory sites. This acquisition is structured as a commercial item purchase under FAR Subpart 12.3 and requires a COTS solution required to be delivered without modification to core application logic. No software development is permitted under this contract. Offerors who propose to build custom modules, develop proprietary integrations requiring new code, or enhance existing product functionality through engineering effort will be found non-compliant. The permissible scope of work is configuration only and includes definition of work breakdown structures, cost account mapping, schedule baseline setup, earned value calculation method selection using product-native options, and report layout customization within the platform's standard reporting environment. The DOE contracting officer's representative will serve as the primary configuration authority and must approve all system settings prior to go-live. A configuration management plan shall be submitted within thirty days of contract award.

The Department of Health and Human Services Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services is conducting a competitive acquisition for an enterprise customer relationship management platform to modernize beneficiary inquiry tracking across regional centers. CMS is committed to a commercial-first technology strategy and accordingly requires that all proposed solutions be fully developed, commercially available products at the time of proposal submission. No custom software development will be authorized or funded under the resulting contract. Vendors shall not propose to develop new features, build custom application layers, or write integration code specific to CMS environments. Configuration only activities are within scope and shall include case queue setup, escalation rule definition, knowledge base population, and API-based integration with existing CMS data systems using pre-built connectors provided by the software vendor. The Government anticipates that some desired capabilities may require process adaptation rather than system customization, and offerors should address this expectation directly in their technical approach. Proposals that substitute development commitments for existing product functionality will receive reduced technical scores.

The Department of Veterans Affairs intends to award a contract for janitorial and custodial services at the Phoenix VA Medical Center. The Government will evaluate offers using the Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) source selection process. Technical acceptability will be determined on a pass/fail basis. Offerors must demonstrate compliance with all technical requirements outlined in the Performance Work Statement. Price will be evaluated for reasonableness and balance. The Government will award a contract to the responsible offeror whose offer conforms to the solicitation requirements and represents the lowest price technically acceptable. No tradeoffs will be made between technical merit and price. Offerors are advised that the Government does not intend to pay a premium for technical proposals that exceed the minimum requirements. Only proposals determined to be technically acceptable will be considered for award. Offerors should not propose capabilities beyond those required, as such additions will not enhance the probability of award under this LPTA evaluation methodology.

This solicitation is issued by the General Services Administration, Public Buildings Service, for grounds maintenance and landscaping services at federal facilities in the National Capital Region. Source selection will be conducted using the Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) methodology as prescribed in FAR 15.101-2. Evaluation factors include Technical Capability and Price. Technical Capability will be rated either Acceptable or Unacceptable based on the offeror's demonstrated ability to meet the requirements of the Statement of Work. Price will not be evaluated for technical merit. Award will be made to the offeror submitting the lowest price among all technically acceptable proposals. Proposals rated Unacceptable under the Technical Capability factor will be eliminated from consideration regardless of price. The Government reserves the right to conduct discussions if necessary but intends to make award without discussions. All offerors must be registered in SAM.gov at time of offer submission.

The Department of Homeland Security, Customs and Border Protection, is soliciting offers for the procurement of personal protective equipment including ballistic helmets, body armor, and tactical gloves for field personnel stationed along the Southwest Border. This acquisition will utilize the lowest price technically acceptable source selection approach. Technical acceptability shall be established through compliance with all mandatory specifications contained in Section C of this solicitation. Compliance with applicable NIJ and ANSI standards is required and will be evaluated on a pass/fail basis. Delivery schedule and applicable certifications must also meet minimum thresholds to be deemed technically acceptable. Among all offers receiving a technically acceptable rating, award will be made to the offeror proposing the lowest price. The Government will not conduct tradeoff analysis. Offerors are strongly encouraged to review all technical requirements prior to submission to ensure their products meet mandatory minimums.

The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Omaha District, is seeking proposals for facility maintenance and repair services at Camp Ashland, Nebraska. The source selection methodology for this procurement is Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA). Offerors must submit a Technical Volume and a Price Volume. The Technical Volume will be evaluated to determine whether the offeror's proposed approach meets the minimum requirements of the Performance Work Statement. Ratings will be limited to Acceptable or Unacceptable. An offeror receiving an Unacceptable technical rating will not be eligible for award. Price will be evaluated for completeness, reasonableness, and balance. Award will be made to the offeror with the lowest evaluated price whose technical proposal has been rated Acceptable. The use of LPTA in this acquisition is consistent with the determination that the requirement is well-defined and risk of unsuccessful performance is minimal. The Government does not anticipate the need for discussions.

The Department of Education, Office of the Chief Information Officer, is requesting proposals for desktop and laptop computer hardware refresh services under this competitive procurement. In accordance with FAR 15.101-2, this acquisition employs the lowest price technically acceptable (LPTA) evaluation method. Technical factors consist of Hardware Specifications Compliance and Delivery Capability, both evaluated on an Acceptable/Unacceptable basis. Offerors must certify that all proposed equipment meets or exceeds the minimum technical specifications listed in Attachment A. Proposals that do not meet all mandatory specifications will be rated Unacceptable and removed from consideration. Among technically acceptable proposals, the Government will select for award the offer representing the lowest total evaluated price. Offerors should not include options or features beyond those required, as additional capabilities will provide no competitive advantage under this LPTA process. All hardware must be TAA compliant and originate from approved sources.

The Naval Facilities Engineering Systems Command, Southwest, hereby solicits offers for refuse collection, recycling, and solid waste management services at Naval Base San Diego. This procurement is conducted using the Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) source selection methodology. Technical evaluation criteria are defined as Acceptable or Unacceptable and include: compliance with all federal, state, and local environmental regulations; demonstration of required equipment and staffing levels; and proof of required licenses and permits. Price evaluation will consider the total evaluated price across the base period and all option years. Award shall be made to the responsible offeror whose proposal is technically acceptable and offers the lowest price. The Government is not seeking best value tradeoffs and will not award to a higher-priced technically acceptable offeror under any circumstances. Offerors must thoroughly address all technical criteria to avoid an Unacceptable rating, which would preclude award regardless of pricing submitted.

The Department of Agriculture, Forest Service, issues this solicitation for tree marking, timber cruising, and silvicultural services in the Tongass National Forest, Alaska. Source selection will be accomplished using the lowest price technically acceptable methodology. The Technical Factor will assess whether the offeror has demonstrated the minimum qualifications, past relevant experience, and equipment necessary to perform the work as described in the Statement of Work. Technical acceptability determinations will be made on a pass/fail basis only. Price will be evaluated as the total price for all contract line items over the ordering period. Among all offerors determined to be technically acceptable, the award will be issued to the offeror presenting the lowest price. The Government has determined that LPTA is appropriate for this acquisition because the work is routine in nature, performance requirements are clearly defined, and successful performance risk is low. Offerors are cautioned that elaborate technical proposals will not improve their competitive standing.

The Social Security Administration, Office of Facilities and Supply Management, is soliciting proposals for courier and document delivery services between SSA regional offices located throughout the Mid-Atlantic region. This solicitation is issued under the Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) source selection method as outlined in FAR 15.101-2. Proposals will be evaluated on two factors: Technical Acceptability and Price. Technical Acceptability will be rated Acceptable or Unacceptable based on whether the offeror demonstrates sufficient vehicle fleet capacity, staffing plans, and route coverage to satisfy the requirements in the Performance Work Statement. No additional credit will be given for exceeding minimum requirements. Price will be evaluated based on the total price across the base year and four option years. Award will be made to the technically acceptable offeror with the lowest evaluated price. Offerors are reminded that submission of a technically acceptable proposal at the lowest price constitutes the sole basis for award determination under this LPTA competition.

The Department of Justice, Bureau of Prisons, is soliciting offers for food service management and inmate meal preparation at the Federal Correctional Institution located in Sheridan, Oregon. The Government has determined that use of the Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) source selection process is appropriate for this procurement. Technical evaluation will consist of two pass/fail subfactors: Staffing Plan Adequacy and Menu Compliance with BOP Nutritional Standards. Offerors must achieve a rating of Acceptable on both subfactors to remain in the competitive range. Price will be evaluated for all contract line items including the base year and four option periods. The Government will award a firm-fixed-price contract to the responsible offeror receiving an Acceptable technical rating and proposing the lowest total evaluated price. The Government expressly declines to conduct a best value tradeoff under this acquisition. All proposals must include completed representations and certifications and signed Standard Form 1449 to be considered responsive.

The Environmental Protection Agency, Office of Acquisition Solutions, is issuing this Request for Proposals for technical support services related to water quality monitoring and sampling at Superfund sites in EPA Region 5. This acquisition will be evaluated using the Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) source selection process in accordance with FAR 15.101-2. Technical acceptability will be evaluated under three pass/fail criteria: Key Personnel Qualifications, Laboratory Accreditation Compliance, and Quality Assurance Plan Adequacy. Proposals must be rated Acceptable on all three criteria to be eligible for award. Technical proposals that exceed minimum requirements will receive no additional consideration. Following determination of technical acceptability, award will be made to the responsible offeror submitting the lowest evaluated total price. The Government has made a written determination that LPTA is the appropriate evaluation approach given the well-defined nature of the work and the availability of qualified contractors capable of performing at minimum standards. Questions regarding the LPTA evaluation criteria must be submitted by the deadline specified in Section L.

This acquisition is a TOTAL SMALL BUSINESS SET-ASIDE in accordance with FAR 19.502-2. Only offers from small business concerns will be considered for award. PROCUREMENT NOTICE — HUBZONE SET-ASIDE:

This requirement is designated as a HUBZone small business set-aside. Pursuant to 15 U.S.C. § 657a and implementing regulations at 13 C.F.R. Part 126, this solicitation is restricted to offerors that are certified HUBZone small business concerns. The Contracting Officer has conducted market research and concluded that the HUBZone set-aside is appropriate given the availability of qualified HUBZone firms in the uniform manufacturing industry. FAR clause 52.219-3, Notice of HUBZone Set-Aside or Sole Source Award, is incorporated into this solicitation. The applicable NAICS code is 315990 with a small business size standard of 500 employees. Firms must hold a valid SBA HUBZone certification and ensure their SAM.gov registration reflects HUBZone eligibility before submitting an offer. Award will be made on a best-value basis. The estimated total value of this requirement is $3.1 million. Non-HUBZone offerors are not eligible to compete and will receive no further consideration upon identification as non-certified during the evaluation process.

SOCIAL SECURITY ADMINISTRATION
Office of Acquisition and Grants
Solicitation: SSA-RFP-24-1147
Program: Administrative Records Management Services

SET-ASIDE DESIGNATION: HUBZone Small Business

The Social Security Administration hereby issues this Request for Proposals as a total set-aside for HUBZone small business concerns under the authority of FAR 19.1305 and the Small Business Act. Only firms with active and valid HUBZone certifications granted by the Small Business Administration are eligible to submit offers in response to this solicitation. Offers received from firms not certified as HUBZone small businesses will be rejected as technically unacceptable without evaluation of price or technical merit. The applicable NAICS code is 518210, Cloud Computing and Data Processing Services, with a size standard of $47.0 million. Offerors must provide their SBA HUBZone certification letter dated within the past twelve months or demonstrate active status in the SBA database. The requirement covers a base year of twelve months with options for four additional twelve-month periods. HUBZone businesses interested in this procurement are encouraged to register on beta.SAM.gov and ensure their HUBZone designation is current. The total estimated value including all option periods is approximately $8.7 million.

DEPARTMENT OF ENERGY
National Energy Technology Laboratory
Solicitation Reference: DE-SOL-0010988
Scope: Mechanical Engineering and Technical Support Services

NOTICE OF HUBZone SET-ASIDE: In accordance with FAR Subpart 19.13 and the Small Business Act, 15 U.S.C. § 657a, this solicitation is being conducted as a competitive HUBZone small business set-aside. The Department of Energy has determined that this requirement is appropriate for the HUBZone program based on current market research indicating the availability of multiple qualified HUBZone small business concerns capable of fulfilling the requirement at fair and reasonable prices. To qualify for this set-aside, firms must be certified as HUBZone small business concerns by the U.S. Small Business Administration. The HUBZone certification must be active and in good standing at the time of proposal submission. NAICS code 541330 (Engineering Services) applies with a size standard of $25.5 million. The Government will independently verify HUBZone certification status through SBA records. FAR 52.219-3 is included in this solicitation. Offerors must also comply with the HUBZone performance of work requirement, ensuring that at least 50% of the contract's labor cost is performed by HUBZone employees. The contract is anticipated to be a cost-plus-fixed-fee type with a period of performance of five years.

The contractor shall implement Agile development methodologies throughout the period of performance. All software development activities shall be conducted using Scrum framework, with two-week sprint cycles. The contractor shall conduct daily stand-up meetings, sprint planning sessions, sprint reviews, and sprint retrospectives in accordance with Scrum ceremonies. A certified Scrum Master shall be designated and responsible for facilitating all Agile ceremonies and removing impediments to team progress. The Government Product Owner shall participate in sprint planning and backlog grooming sessions to prioritize user stories and acceptance criteria. The contractor shall maintain a product backlog in a Government-approved project management tool and provide sprint velocity metrics as part of monthly progress reporting. All deliverables shall be produced incrementally, with a potentially shippable product increment demonstrated at the conclusion of each sprint. The contractor shall adhere to the Agile Manifesto principles and demonstrate continuous improvement through retrospective action items documented and tracked across sprints.

The Department of Veterans Affairs requires the selected vendor to employ Agile/Scrum methodology for all development efforts under this task order. The contractor shall organize development personnel into cross-functional Agile teams, each consisting of a Scrum Master, developers, testers, and business analysts. Sprint duration shall be fixed at two weeks, and the contractor shall deliver working software at the end of each sprint for Government review and acceptance. The contractor shall conduct sprint reviews with VA stakeholders to demonstrate completed user stories against agreed-upon Definition of Done criteria. Backlog refinement sessions shall occur at minimum once per sprint to ensure the product backlog reflects current priorities and is sufficiently groomed for upcoming sprints. The contractor shall provide release planning documentation outlining the expected delivery of features across program increments. Agile metrics including sprint burndown charts, velocity, and cumulative flow diagrams shall be reported to the Contracting Officer's Representative on a bi-weekly basis.

In accordance with the Department of Homeland Security's Digital Services Strategy, all software engineering work performed under this contract shall follow Scaled Agile Framework (SAFe) practices. The contractor shall conduct Program Increment (PI) Planning events every ten weeks, bringing together all Agile Release Train teams to align on objectives and identify dependencies. Each Agile team shall operate in two-week iterations with clearly defined iteration goals established during iteration planning ceremonies. The contractor shall designate Release Train Engineers to facilitate ART-level events and support teams in removing cross-team impediments. The System Demo, conducted at the end of each PI, shall be open to all Government stakeholders and demonstrate integrated working software. The contractor shall maintain and update Team and Program backlogs within the Government's approved Agile lifecycle management tooling. Inspect and Adapt workshops shall be conducted at the close of each PI to drive continuous improvement and quantify the improvement backlog.

This Performance Work Statement requires the contractor to execute all software design, development, and testing activities using Agile Scrum methodology in strict compliance with the agency's DevSecOps and Agile Transformation Initiative. The contractor shall establish and maintain Agile teams with a recommended team size of five to nine members, each including a dedicated Scrum Master and supported by a Government-designated Product Owner. User stories shall be written with clearly defined acceptance criteria using the standard 'As a [user], I want [functionality], so that [benefit]' format and shall be estimated using Planning Poker or equivalent relative sizing techniques. The contractor shall operate on a consistent two-week sprint cadence and shall not alter sprint scope once the sprint has commenced without documented consent from the Contracting Officer's Representative. Sprint retrospective outputs shall be shared with the Government and incorporated into the team's continuous improvement plan. Quarterly roadmaps reflecting planned feature delivery shall be maintained and updated following each sprint review.

The General Services Administration Office of Information Technology requires offerors to demonstrate proven experience delivering projects using Agile development practices. The period of performance shall be executed through iterative Agile sprints of two-week duration. The contractor shall implement all five Scrum events: Sprint Planning, Daily Scrum, Sprint Review, Sprint Retrospective, and the Sprint itself, with Government personnel invited and encouraged to participate in Sprint Reviews and Retrospectives. The contractor's Agile Coach shall work with the Government Product Owner to build and maintain a prioritized product backlog, ensuring that the highest-value features are delivered earliest. Acceptance testing shall be performed within the sprint in which development occurs, consistent with Agile testing best practices and shift-left quality principles. The contractor shall track and report on Agile health metrics including team happiness index, sprint goal achievement rate, and escaped defect rate. All tools used to manage the Agile process, including the backlog and sprint boards, shall be accessible to designated Government personnel at all times.

The Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services requires the contractor to apply Agile methodology across all workstreams supporting the modernization of legacy claims processing systems. The contractor shall organize work into user stories, epics, and themes within a hierarchical backlog structure and shall demonstrate traceability from individual user stories to program-level epics and strategic objectives. Sprints shall be conducted on a two-week cadence with sprint planning occurring on the first day of each sprint and sprint review and retrospective conducted on the final day. The Government's designated Product Owner shall have authority to accept or reject sprint deliverables based on the agreed Definition of Done. The contractor shall not carry incomplete user stories across sprint boundaries without documented justification and re-estimation. The contractor shall facilitate quarterly Agile maturity assessments using a Government-approved framework and shall present findings and improvement recommendations to the CMS program office. Test-Driven Development (TDD) and Continuous Integration practices shall be employed as integral components of the Agile engineering workflow.

Pursuant to the Department of Defense's software acquisition pathway as defined in DoDI 5000.87, the contractor shall deliver capabilities using Agile software development methodology with continuous integration and continuous delivery pipelines. The contractor shall organize personnel into Agile squads operating in two-week sprints, with each squad responsible for a defined set of product backlog items. All Agile ceremonies, including sprint planning, daily standups, sprint reviews, and retrospectives, shall be conducted in person or via Government-approved collaboration tools. The contractor shall employ a certified Scrum Master for each squad who is accountable for coaching team members on Agile principles and ensuring adherence to Scrum practices. A product roadmap updated at minimum quarterly shall be maintained and shared with Government stakeholders to provide visibility into planned increments of capability. The use of Kanban boards or equivalent visual management tools is required to provide real-time transparency into work-in-progress, blocked items, and completed work. Sprint velocity shall be tracked and used to inform release planning and capacity projections.

The Social Security Administration requires all IT development work performed under this Blanket Purchase Agreement to comply with SSA's Agile Development Standards. Offerors shall describe their Agile delivery model in the technical approach and demonstrate how their proposed methodology aligns with Scrum or Kanban frameworks. Contractors shall conduct sprint planning meetings with SSA's Product Owner to decompose epics into sprint-ready user stories with acceptance criteria no later than the iteration planning session preceding each sprint. The contractor shall update the Government's enterprise project management tool daily with sprint board status, impediments, and burndown data. Demonstration of working software to SSA stakeholders shall occur at the end of every sprint, and the Government reserves the right to redirect backlog priorities following each sprint review based on mission needs. The contractor shall implement Definition of Ready criteria to ensure user stories are sufficiently detailed prior to sprint commitment. Quarterly business reviews shall include Agile performance metrics, including average sprint velocity, defect density, and sprint goal completion percentage.

The Department of Education, Office of the Chief Information Officer, requires the contractor to lead and execute a modernization initiative using Agile Scrum practices in alignment with the agency's Technology Modernization Playbook. The contractor shall staff an Agile delivery team that includes at minimum a Scrum Master, Product Manager, UX Researcher, Front-End and Back-End Developers, and a Quality Assurance Engineer. All work shall be organized into a continuously refined product backlog with epics, features, and user stories sized and prioritized by the Government Product Owner in collaboration with the contractor. Sprint cycles shall be two weeks in duration, and the contractor shall present a sprint demo to agency stakeholders at each sprint review, with attendance tracked and documented. The Scrum Master shall facilitate sprint retrospectives and shall maintain a retrospective action log accessible to the Government. Agile metrics shall be reported in the contractor's monthly status report, including planned versus completed story points, sprint goal achievement rate, and the number of impediments raised and resolved. The contractor shall provide Agile coaching support to Government personnel as a secondary deliverable to build internal agency capacity.

Under this task order issued against the NASA Solutions for Enterprise-Wide Procurement contract, the contractor shall manage and execute all software development activities in accordance with Agile methodology. The contractor shall implement a Scrum-based framework and shall operate all development teams on a uniform two-week sprint cadence synchronized with the program's release train schedule. Sprint planning shall be completed within four hours for a two-week sprint, and the contractor shall ensure that sprint goals are clearly articulated and agreed upon by the Government Product Owner prior to sprint commencement. The contractor shall maintain a Definition of Done that includes code review completion, unit test coverage thresholds, security scanning, and Product Owner acceptance. Working software shall be deployed to a staging environment at the end of each sprint for Government validation. The contractor shall produce and maintain an Agile project charter documenting team norms, Agile ceremony schedules, tooling agreements, and escalation procedures. Release planning artifacts including feature roadmaps and program increment objectives shall be reviewed and approved by the NASA Contracting Officer's Representative on a quarterly basis.

The Government reserves the right to conduct oral presentations with offerors in the competitive range. Oral presentations will be scheduled following the initial evaluation of written proposals. Each offeror shall be prepared to present and discuss their technical approach, management strategy, and key personnel qualifications before a panel of Government evaluators. The oral presentation shall not exceed ninety (90) minutes, inclusive of a question-and-answer period not to exceed thirty (30) minutes. Offerors may use visual aids such as slides or diagrams; however, all materials presented during the oral presentation must be submitted in writing to the Contracting Officer no later than 48 hours prior to the scheduled session. Information disclosed during the oral presentation will be considered part of the offeror's proposal and will be evaluated under the Technical Approach and Key Personnel factors. The Government will not compensate offerors for costs associated with preparation or participation in oral presentations. Specific scheduling instructions will be provided to offerors selected for the competitive range via written notification from the Contracting Officer.

Section M – Evaluation Criteria: The Department of Homeland Security will evaluate all responsive proposals using a Best Value Tradeoff methodology. Following written proposal evaluation, offerors deemed within the competitive range will be invited to deliver an oral presentation to the Source Selection Evaluation Board (SSEB). The oral presentation is intended to allow offerors to clarify and elaborate upon their written technical submissions and to demonstrate the depth of understanding of the program requirements. Presentations shall be limited to sixty (60) minutes with an additional twenty (20) minutes reserved for Government questions. The lead proposed Program Manager and at least two key technical personnel identified in the proposal must participate in person. Remote participation via video teleconference will be permitted only upon written approval by the Contracting Officer. Scores assigned during the oral presentation phase will be integrated with written proposal scores to produce a final technical rating for each offeror.

The National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) anticipates conducting oral presentations as a component of the proposal evaluation process for this acquisition. Offerors should be prepared to present their Mission Suitability approach, including their understanding of technical requirements, proposed solutions, and risk mitigation strategies. The oral presentation will be evaluated under the Mission Suitability factor and will contribute to the overall technical score. NASA will notify offerors in the competitive range of their scheduled presentation date and time no fewer than five (5) business days in advance. Each presentation session shall not exceed two (2) hours. The presenting team must include the proposed Principal Investigator and Project Manager. Offerors are advised that statements made during the oral presentation are binding and will be incorporated into the resulting contract. Any commitments made verbally that differ from the written proposal will be flagged for clarification prior to award. Visual aids and demonstrations may be used but must be pre-approved by the Contracting Officer.

Offerors in the competitive range shall be required to participate in an oral presentation as part of the evaluation process. The oral presentation will be conducted at the Department of Veterans Affairs Network Contracting Office and will be scheduled at the Government's discretion. The purpose of the oral presentation is to assess each offeror's understanding of the Performance Work Statement requirements, proposed staffing model, and quality control methodology. Presentations shall be a maximum of seventy-five (75) minutes in duration, which includes a mandatory fifteen (15) minute question-and-answer session conducted by the Technical Evaluation Panel. The proposed Contract Program Manager must attend and lead the presentation. Supporting staff may participate but shall not exceed four (4) individuals total. All slide materials or visual aids must be provided to the Contracting Officer no later than three (3) business days prior to the presentation. The Government may record oral presentations for evaluation documentation purposes, and offerors will be notified accordingly.

As part of the evaluation methodology for this Information Technology Services procurement, the General Services Administration (GSA) will incorporate oral presentations into the source selection process. After an initial review of written proposals, offerors receiving a rating of Acceptable or higher under the Technical Factor will be invited to participate in an oral presentation before the Technical Evaluation Team. The oral presentation allows the Government to assess offeror capabilities beyond what is conveyed in written form and to evaluate proposed personnel directly. Each offeror will be allotted ninety (90) minutes total: sixty (60) minutes for the presentation and thirty (30) minutes for Government-directed questions. Key personnel named in Section H must be present and actively participate. Substitutions of key personnel for the oral presentation will not be permitted without prior written approval and may negatively impact the evaluation. Offerors should note that the oral presentation is evaluated separately under Factor 2 – Management Approach and scored on an Adjectival basis consistent with the criteria described in Section M.

The Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) intends to conduct oral presentations with offerors as part of its evaluation of proposals submitted in response to this solicitation for Environmental Monitoring and Assessment Support Services. Oral presentations will be conducted following the initial screening of written proposals. Only those offerors determined to be technically acceptable after written review will be scheduled for an oral presentation. The oral presentation will focus primarily on the offeror's technical methodology, data management approach, and the qualifications of proposed scientific personnel. Each session is limited to one hundred and twenty (120) minutes, including a thirty (30) minute question period. The proposed Principal Investigator must personally attend and lead the oral presentation. Additional team members may participate at the offeror's discretion but are subject to a five-person maximum. Materials presented must not introduce new technical approaches not previously described in the written proposal, and any inconsistencies identified will be evaluated unfavorably.

In accordance with FAR 15.102, this solicitation incorporates oral presentations as a substitute for, or supplement to, written information for the Technical Approach evaluation factor. Offerors selected for oral presentations will receive written notification from the Contracting Officer specifying the date, time, location, and format requirements. The Department of Energy's Office of Environmental Management will evaluate the oral presentation under Subfactor 1A – Technical Understanding and Subfactor 1B – Staffing and Organization. Each offeror's presentation team must include the proposed Project Director, Deputy Project Director, and at least one subject matter expert for each major technical area described in the Statement of Work. Presentations shall not exceed one hundred and fifty (150) minutes inclusive of Government questions. Offerors are encouraged to use visual aids, technical demonstrations, or mock deliverable examples to enhance their presentations. The evaluation panel will score the oral presentation independently from written submissions, and both scores will be combined to produce the final Technical Approach rating used in the Best Value determination.

The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) will conduct oral presentations with competitive range offerors as an integral component of the source selection evaluation for this Investigative Support Services contract. Oral presentations are designed to enable the Government to evaluate the offeror's technical capabilities, proposed methodologies, and the experience of key personnel in a direct interactive format. The FBI will schedule oral presentations at Headquarters in Washington, D.C., and travel costs shall be borne by the offeror. Each presentation shall be strictly limited to sixty (60) minutes, followed by a thirty (30) minute question-and-answer period facilitated by the Source Selection Advisory Council. The offeror's proposed Program Manager must lead the presentation and respond to technical questions. All materials used during the oral presentation must be classified no higher than the level specified in the DD Form 254 attached hereto. Offerors must submit all presentation materials through the designated secure portal no later than seventy-two (72) hours prior to their scheduled session. Evaluation results from the oral presentation will be documented and retained as part of the official source selection record.

Proposals submitted in response to this solicitation will undergo a multi-phase evaluation process. Phase I consists of written technical and price proposal review. Offerors achieving a combined written evaluation score that places them within the competitive range will advance to Phase II, which requires an oral presentation before the Department of Transportation Technical Review Board. The oral presentation phase is designed to supplement written proposals by allowing offerors to demonstrate technical depth, address evaluator questions regarding proposed approaches, and introduce key personnel who will execute the contract. Oral presentations will be evaluated under Factor 1 – Technical Approach and Factor 3 – Key Personnel, and scores derived from the oral presentation will be weighted equally with written proposal scores for those factors. Each presentation is allocated ninety (90) minutes, with no more than sixty (60) minutes reserved for the offeror's presentation and the remainder designated for Government questions. The offeror's Project Manager and at least two senior technical staff members named in the proposal must be physically present.

This solicitation for Healthcare Information Technology Modernization Support Services includes an oral presentation requirement as a formal element of the evaluation process. The Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) will invite offerors in the competitive range to participate in structured oral presentations following the initial written proposal review. Oral presentations will be assessed under Technical Factor 2 – Implementation Methodology and Technical Factor 4 – Relevant Experience. Each offeror will have seventy-five (75) minutes to present, followed by a twenty-five (25) minute question-and-answer session conducted by the HHS Evaluation Committee. The offeror's proposed Program Manager, Lead Systems Architect, and at least one clinical informatics specialist must be present in person. Remote participation is not authorized except under emergency circumstances approved in advance by the Contracting Officer. Offerors should structure their oral presentation to directly address the evaluation criteria outlined in Section M and are advised that new information introduced during the oral presentation that was not contained in the written proposal will be evaluated as a clarification and not as a revision or amendment to the offer.

The Department of Transportation invites qualified vendors to participate in a mandatory design challenge as part of this solicitation process. Offerors shall develop and submit a working prototype of their proposed traffic management interface within thirty (30) calendar days of solicitation release. The prototype must demonstrate core functionality including real-time data ingestion, alert escalation workflows, and dashboard visualization. Government evaluators will conduct hands-on testing of each submitted prototype during a structured demonstration session scheduled at DOT headquarters in Washington, D.C. Scoring criteria for the design challenge component account for forty percent (40%) of the overall technical evaluation score. Prototypes that fail to meet minimum functionality thresholds as defined in Attachment C will be deemed technically unacceptable and removed from further consideration. Offerors are encouraged to review the Technical Evaluation Criteria in Section M prior to initiating prototype development. All prototype submissions must be delivered via secure government portal no later than 5:00 PM Eastern Time on the date specified in the solicitation timeline.

As part of the technical evaluation process, the National Institutes of Health requires each offeror to complete a hands-on exercise designed to assess the team's proficiency in bioinformatics pipeline development. During the oral presentation phase, offeror technical staff will be given a standardized genomic dataset and asked to demonstrate live data processing, variant calling, and results visualization using their proposed toolchain. This exercise will be conducted in a controlled government environment; offerors may not bring pre-configured scripts or pre-processed outputs. Evaluators will observe the exercise in real time and score performance based on accuracy, efficiency, and team collaboration as outlined in Section M.3. The hands-on exercise is mandatory for all offerors advancing past the initial written proposal review. Failure to participate in or successfully complete the exercise will result in disqualification from award consideration. NIH reserves the right to modify the dataset provided during the exercise without prior notice to ensure evaluation integrity.

The General Services Administration hereby issues this solicitation which includes a required design exercise component for all competitive range offerors. Specifically, each offeror must submit a prototype submission demonstrating their proposed citizen-facing web portal solution. The prototype submission shall be a fully navigable, hosted demonstration environment accessible via government-provided credentials. The prototype must include functional user registration, document upload, and status tracking modules. GSA evaluators will engage directly with the prototype during a structured two-hour evaluation session and will simulate end-user workflows as described in Attachment D, User Scenario Matrix. Prototype submissions are due no later than fourteen (14) days prior to the scheduled oral presentations. Offerors are advised that the prototype submission will be evaluated independently from the written technical volume and carries a weight of thirty-five percent (35%) of the total technical score. Any prototype that is unavailable, incomplete, or non-functional during the evaluation session will receive a rating of Unacceptable for that factor.

The Department of Homeland Security, Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency, requires offerors to participate in a structured design challenge as a condition of this competitive acquisition. The design challenge will task each offeror's technical team with developing a threat detection use case scenario using a simulated network environment provided by the Government. Offerors will have four (4) hours to architect a detection logic framework, implement rules within the provided SIEM platform, and produce a written summary of their methodology. The design challenge is intended to evaluate offeror capability in applied threat intelligence, rule engineering, and documentation quality under realistic operational conditions. Government evaluators will assess outputs using a standardized rubric detailed in Section M, Evaluation Criteria. The design challenge will be conducted at a CISA facility; specific location and scheduling details will be provided to competitive range offerors via the Contracting Officer. Teams may consist of no more than four (4) individuals. All work product generated during the exercise becomes the property of the United States Government upon submission.

In accordance with the evaluation methodology described herein, the Department of Veterans Affairs requires each offeror to deliver a prototype submission of their proposed electronic health record integration module. The prototype submission shall demonstrate bidirectional data exchange between the offeror's platform and a VA-supplied test instance of the existing legacy system. Offerors must configure and host the prototype in a government-authorized cloud environment prior to the evaluation period. VA technical evaluators will conduct a structured review session lasting no more than three (3) hours, during which they will execute pre-defined integration test scripts and evaluate system responses. The prototype submission will be used to validate claims made in the written technical proposal; any discrepancies between the written proposal and demonstrated prototype functionality may result in a downward adjustment to the technical rating. Offerors must submit prototype access credentials to the Contracting Officer no fewer than five (5) business days before the scheduled evaluation session. Detailed instructions for the VA test environment configuration are provided in Section J, Attachment 7.

The National Aeronautics and Space Administration solicits proposals for advanced telemetry processing software and, as part of this acquisition, mandates a design challenge for all offerors in the competitive range. The design challenge will require offeror teams to receive a raw telemetry data stream, process it using their proposed software architecture, and produce a formatted output file meeting specifications outlined in Appendix F within a two-hour window. NASA evaluators will monitor all aspects of the exercise and score submissions based on data fidelity, processing speed, error handling, and output compliance. Offerors may use their own laptops and licensed software tools but may not access external networks during the exercise. The design challenge is specifically intended to assess whether proposed technical approaches translate into functional capability under realistic mission constraints. Results of the design challenge will be incorporated into the Mission Suitability technical factor score and weighted at twenty-five percent (25%) of that factor.
