NASA reserves the right to modify the complexity of the telemetry dataset to ensure differentiation among offerors.

The Environmental Protection Agency, Office of Environmental Information, requires a hands-on exercise as a component of the technical evaluation for this Blanket Purchase Agreement competition. Each offeror will be given access to a standardized environmental monitoring dataset and tasked with producing a data quality analysis report and visualization dashboard within a three-hour period. The hands-on exercise will be conducted at EPA's Research Triangle Park facility. Offerors must bring all necessary hardware and licensed software; EPA will provide only network connectivity and access to the standardized dataset via a secure file transfer mechanism. Evaluators will assess the quality, accuracy, and clarity of the analysis report and dashboard outputs using criteria specified in Section M.4. The hands-on exercise score will be combined with written technical proposal scores to produce a composite technical rating. Offerors are advised that strong written proposals will not compensate for poor performance on the hands-on exercise, as the Agency views demonstrated capability as equally important to proposed methodology. All outputs produced during the exercise must be submitted to the Contracting Officer's Representative before participants depart the facility.

The Department of Defense, Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency, hereby solicits proposals under this Broad Agency Announcement and requires all offerors selected for Phase II negotiations to complete a mandatory prototype submission. The prototype submission shall constitute a functional hardware or software artifact demonstrating feasibility of the proposed technical approach as described in Section 3.2 of this solicitation. DARPA program managers will evaluate each prototype submission during a dedicated review session, which may include independent laboratory testing, performance benchmarking, and direct technical questioning of the offeror team. The prototype submission evaluation will assess innovation, technical merit, and readiness level advancement relative to the baseline technology described in the solicitation background. Offerors must ship hardware prototypes to the designated government laboratory no fewer than ten (10) business days prior to the evaluation session. Software prototypes must be delivered via approved media and must execute without modification in the government-provided test environment. DARPA evaluators retain the right to attempt to replicate offeror-reported performance results using independent test procedures.

The Department of Education, Office of Educational Technology, requires offerors responding to this Request for Proposals to submit materials for a design exercise evaluating user experience and instructional design capability. Each offeror must develop and submit a prototype submission consisting of a fully functional two-module eLearning course built to SCORM 2004 standards, targeting the competencies specified in Attachment B, Learning Objectives Matrix. The prototype submission must be operable within the Government's existing Learning Management System, access credentials for which will be provided to offerors upon solicitation release. Evaluators will enroll as learners in each submitted course and complete all activities, assessments, and interactions. Scoring will address instructional alignment, user interface design, accessibility compliance under Section 508, and technical performance within the LMS environment. The design exercise prototype accounts for thirty percent (30%) of the Technical Approach factor score. Offerors are advised that the prototype submission should be representative of the quality and methodology they intend to apply throughout the base and option periods of performance. Submissions that fail Section 508 compliance checks will be rated no higher than Marginal for the design exercise component.

The Department of Housing and Urban Development issues this solicitation for data analytics platform services and requires, as part of the competitive evaluation, that offerors participate in a structured design challenge demonstrating their platform's analytical capabilities. The design challenge will provide each offeror with a HUD-supplied housing market dataset containing deliberately introduced data quality issues, missing records, and anomalous entries. Offeror teams will have five (5) hours to cleanse the dataset, perform trend analysis, develop predictive model outputs, and present findings in a final briefing to HUD evaluators. The design challenge is intended to replicate conditions representative of actual task order performance and will be evaluated on analytical rigor, interpretive accuracy, visualization quality, and presentation clarity. No more than five (5) offeror personnel may participate in the design challenge. HUD will provide workstations loaded with a baseline software suite; offerors wishing to use additional licensed tools must request approval from the Contracting Officer no fewer than seven (7) days before the scheduled exercise date. Results will directly inform the Technical Capability and Staffing evaluation factors as described in Section M.

The Department of Veterans Affairs, Office of Information and Technology, hereby solicits proposals for the development and deployment of a patient scheduling optimization tool for use at one regional VA medical center. The total ceiling for this contract shall not exceed $85,000 for a base period of performance of twelve (12) months. No option periods are anticipated at this time. Offerors are advised that all technical deliverables, including requirements documentation, system design, development, testing, training, and post-deployment support, must be accomplished within the stated ceiling amount. The Government does not intend to award a contract exceeding this threshold under any circumstances. This acquisition is set aside exclusively for small businesses under NAICS code 541511. Interested parties should note that the contracting officer has determined that $85,000 represents a fair and reasonable price for the described scope of work based on independent government cost estimates. Proposals that exceed this maximum budget will be deemed non-responsive and will not be evaluated further. Offerors must clearly demonstrate how all required tasks can be completed within the $85,000 total contract value.

SOLICITATION NUMBER: EPA-R5-2024-0042. The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency, Region 5, is seeking a qualified contractor to conduct a Phase I Environmental Site Assessment and prepare a corresponding written report for a brownfield site located in metropolitan Chicago, Illinois. The firm-fixed-price contract resulting from this solicitation will carry a total obligated value of $47,500. This amount is inclusive of all labor, travel, laboratory analysis coordination, report preparation, and incidental expenses. No additional funding is available, and the Government will not negotiate modifications to increase the contract ceiling. The period of performance is ninety (90) calendar days from date of award. This procurement is reserved for businesses certified under the 8(a) Business Development Program. The independent government cost estimate supporting this solicitation was developed in accordance with EPA acquisition policy and confirms that $47,500 is commensurate with current market rates for equivalent assessment services. All costs associated with the delivery of final work products must fall within the stated contract amount.

The National Park Service, Intermountain Region, issues this Request for Proposals for interpretive signage design and fabrication services at a designated historic district within Capitol Reef National Park. The Government anticipates awarding a single firm-fixed-price purchase order with a total value not to exceed $62,000. This figure encompasses all design labor, graphic production, materials, fabrication, site delivery, and installation activities. The NPS has conducted market research confirming that the described scope of work is achievable within this funding level. Offerors that cannot perform all required tasks within the $62,000 ceiling are encouraged not to submit proposals, as the agency has no flexibility to increase this award amount. The period of performance is one hundred and twenty (120) days after issuance of the purchase order. This acquisition is 100% set aside for small businesses. Quoters must provide a detailed cost breakdown demonstrating that total proposed costs, inclusive of all direct and indirect expenses and profit or fee, do not surpass sixty-two thousand dollars ($62,000).

REQUEST FOR QUOTATION – DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION, OFFICE OF INSPECTOR GENERAL. The OIG seeks quotations for professional transcription and translation services in support of investigative activities across a six-month base ordering period. A single Blanket Purchase Agreement will be established with one vendor. The maximum spend authority under the BPA shall be limited to $75,000 for the base period. Calls placed against this agreement may not in aggregate exceed this ceiling, and no modification will be pursued to raise the authorized funding level. The Government estimates approximately 1,200 hours of transcription and 300 pages of certified translation will be required, though actual order volumes may vary. Vendors must confirm in their quotations that their proposed unit rates, when applied against these estimated quantities, yield a total that falls within the $75,000 maximum. Any vendor whose rates result in a projected total cost exceeding the available funding will not be considered for award. This solicitation is open to all small businesses holding current GSA Schedule 36 contracts.

The Small Business Administration, Office of Disaster Assistance, requires data entry and document processing support services for its Fort Worth, Texas processing center. This acquisition is structured as a Time-and-Materials task order to be placed against an existing SBA Indefinite Delivery contract. The total funded amount for this task order is $93,000, representing the maximum labor and other direct cost reimbursement available. The Government has budgeted no contingency above this figure. Offerors must propose labor categories and hourly rates that, when multiplied by the estimated 2,400 hours of performance, do not exceed the $93,000 ceiling inclusive of all applicable materials and ODCs. The period of performance is six months. Proposals must include a detailed staffing plan and a cost narrative demonstrating how all tasks will be completed within the ninety-three thousand dollar ($93,000) total funded value. Submissions exceeding this ceiling will be rejected without further evaluation.

SOLICITATION: USDA-AMS-2024-MKTG-007. The United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Marketing Service, requests proposals for the development of a stakeholder outreach and communications plan to support the implementation of updated organic certification standards. The award will be a firm-fixed-price contract with a total contract value of fifty-five thousand dollars ($55,000), inclusive of all deliverables across the eight-month period of performance. Funding for this effort was appropriated under the AMS Market Development Program and has been identified and confirmed in that amount. The agency conducted an independent government estimate concluding that $55,000 is appropriate for the scope described in this solicitation. Offerors are put on notice that the Government cannot and will not augment this funding under any circumstances, including through supplemental agreements or modifications. A detailed work breakdown structure and cost justification must accompany each proposal, and proposed costs must not exceed the fifty-five thousand dollar total. Proposals offering reduced scope in exchange for lower pricing are not acceptable.

The General Services Administration, Federal Acquisition Service, Region 6, issues this combined synopsis and solicitation for janitorial and custodial services at a single federal office building in Kansas City, Missouri, containing approximately 8,400 rentable square feet. The contract will be structured as a firm-fixed-price arrangement for a base year with no options. The maximum contract value, inclusive of all labor, supplies, equipment, and supervision for the twelve-month base period, is $38,500. This figure reflects the agency's available appropriations for facility maintenance services at this location. Prospective vendors must be capable of providing five-day-per-week service within this funding constraint. The GSA contracting officer has documented that market research supports the feasibility of fulfilling the described services within the $38,500 ceiling based on comparable awards at similar-sized facilities. Quoters whose proposed annual cost exceeds thirty-eight thousand five hundred dollars ($38,500) will be eliminated from consideration. Award will be made on a lowest-price, technically acceptable basis.

Department of Homeland Security, Transportation Security Administration, hereby solicits offers for a limited-scope human factors usability study of proposed checkpoint signage modifications at a single mid-sized domestic airport. The resulting contract will be firm-fixed-price with a total obligated value not to exceed $69,000. This includes all labor for study design, participant recruitment coordination, data collection, analysis, and final report preparation. The TSA Office of Security Capabilities has confirmed that $69,000 represents the entirety of funding allocated for this effort under the current fiscal year research budget, and no carryover or supplemental appropriation is anticipated. The period of performance is five months from date of award. Proposals must include a full technical approach and a price proposal that does not exceed sixty-nine thousand dollars ($69,000) in total. Offerors should note that this is a hard ceiling, not an estimated cost, and any proposal with a total price above $69,000 will be removed from the competitive range and receive no further consideration.

NOTICE OF INTENT TO AWARD / REQUEST FOR PROPOSALS – DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE, BUREAU OF PRISONS. The Federal Bureau of Prisons, Facilities Management Branch, solicits proposals for the repair and restoration of exterior masonry at one medium-security correctional facility in the Southeast Region. The scope includes inspection, joint repointing, surface cleaning, and application of waterproof sealant across approximately 4,000 square feet of affected surface area. This procurement will result in a single award firm-fixed-price contract. Available funding for this effort totals $88,000, which constitutes the full and complete contract ceiling. The BOP has determined that this project must be executed within that amount based on current capital improvement budget allocations for the facility. No modifications adding additional scope or funding will be entertained after award. Contractors must include a complete material and labor cost breakdown with their proposals and certify that the total proposed price, inclusive of all overhead, profit, bonding, and insurance, does not exceed eighty-eight thousand dollars ($88,000). Proposals above this ceiling will be rejected.

The Department of Commerce, National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, Ocean Service, seeks a qualified vendor to provide professional editing, layout, and print-ready production services for NOAA's annual State of the Coast report, encompassing approximately 120 pages of technical narrative, infographics, and photography. This acquisition will be executed as a firm-fixed-price purchase order. The maximum obligated amount for these services is $41,200, which covers all editorial labor, graphic design, accessibility compliance review, and file preparation across the six-month period of performance. NOAA has no additional discretionary funds available for this publication and has structured the scope of work specifically to be executable within the forty-one thousand two hundred dollar ($41,200) ceiling. Vendors must propose pricing for all services described in the statement of work, and total proposed pricing must fall at or below $41,200. Offerors are discouraged from proposing optional enhancements or add-on services that would increase cost above the available budget. Award will be made to the lowest-priced technically acceptable offeror whose total price is within the stated funding constraint.

The Department of Veterans Affairs seeks a qualified contractor to assume responsibility for the continuing work on the Veterans Benefits Management System (VBMS). The selected vendor will inherit the existing system architecture, currently maintained by the incumbent contractor whose period of performance concludes on September 30, 2024. The incoming contractor must demonstrate proven experience onboarding into a legacy codebase without service disruption. Transition activities shall include a mandatory 90-day knowledge transfer period during which the outgoing and incoming contractors will operate concurrently. The Government requires that the new contractor review all existing technical documentation, data dictionaries, and system design records prior to assuming full operational responsibility. Offerors must describe in their technical approach how they will manage risk during transition from incumbent operations, including how they will handle undocumented components of the existing system. Continuity of service to Veterans during the transition period is a non-negotiable requirement, and the Government will evaluate all proposals on the basis of transition risk mitigation strategies.

The Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) is issuing this solicitation for Information Technology development and maintenance services in support of the Medicare Appeals System (MAS). The selected contractor will be continuing work initiated under Contract Number HHSM-500-2018-00012I and must be prepared to take full ownership of the legacy codebase, which includes approximately 1.2 million lines of Java and Python code developed over the past six years. The contractor must conduct a comprehensive code audit within the first 60 days of contract award. CMS acknowledges that portions of the existing system rely on deprecated libraries and outdated middleware components; the contractor's technical approach must address a modernization roadmap while preserving current functionality. All intellectual property associated with the existing system shall be transitioned to the incoming contractor under a Government-Furnished Information package provided at contract award.

The Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) hereby solicits proposals for the continued development and sustainment of the National Flood Insurance Program's Policy Management Platform. This acquisition represents a transition from incumbent contractor DXC Technology, whose base period of performance expires in the fourth quarter of Fiscal Year 2025. The incoming contractor will be responsible for taking over an existing system that processes in excess of five million policyholder records annually. The Government has prepared a Transition Plan framework that the selected contractor must execute, including the migration of all active development branches, issue tracking records, and CI/CD pipeline configurations. Offerors are advised that the existing system was built on a microservices architecture deployed on Amazon Web Services GovCloud and that familiarity with this environment is expected. The technical evaluation criteria will weight heavily the offeror's demonstrated experience assuming operational control of complex legacy systems under compressed timelines.

The Social Security Administration (SSA) announces a competitive procurement for modernization and operations support services for the Disability Case Processing System (DCPS). Contractors responding to this solicitation must understand that this effort constitutes continuing work on a system first placed into production in 2016. The existing codebase, comprising front-end Angular components and back-end services built on IBM WebSphere, will be transferred to the incoming contractor in its current state. SSA does not anticipate a full redesign during the base period; rather, the contractor is expected to maintain, enhance, and incrementally modernize the existing system in accordance with the Technology Modernization Plan attached as Exhibit C. The Government will facilitate a structured transition from incumbent contractor Leidos, providing access to all repository assets, architectural documentation, and operations runbooks. Proposals must include a staffing plan that accounts for immediate knowledge absorption from outgoing personnel during a 120-day transition period.

The Department of Transportation, Federal Aviation Administration (FAA), requires professional services to support the Air Traffic Organization's En Route Automation Modernization (ERAM) program. This solicitation covers continuing work on operational software that is currently deployed across 20 Air Route Traffic Control Centers nationwide. Contractors must have the technical capacity to engage with a mature legacy codebase written predominantly in Ada and C++, with some components dating to original system development in the late 1990s. The FAA will facilitate a formal transition from the incumbent program support contractor; however, the incoming team must be prepared for gaps in documentation that are common in systems of this age and operational complexity. Offerors must demonstrate past performance on programs of comparable scale where they have successfully taken over an existing system with safety-critical operational requirements. All proposed personnel in key positions must hold current FAA suitability determinations or equivalent federal security clearances at time of award.

The National Institutes of Health (NIH), Office of Research Services, issues this Request for Proposal for the operation and enhancement of the NIH Enterprise Directory (NED) and associated Identity Management infrastructure. This procurement represents a direct transition from incumbent contractor Booz Allen Hamilton and requires the selected offeror to assume stewardship of the existing system within 90 calendar days of contract award. The existing system integrates with more than 40 downstream NIH applications and processes identity lifecycle events for over 100,000 active users across the NIH enterprise. The incoming contractor must perform a full inventory of integration points within the first 30 days of the transition period. NIH will provide Government-Furnished Equipment and access to existing system repositories hosted in NIH's internal GitLab instance. Proposals must describe in detail the offeror's methodology for continuing work while simultaneously identifying technical debt and recommending a prioritized remediation schedule for submission to the Contracting Officer's Representative no later than Day 60 of the transition.

The General Services Administration (GSA) Federal Acquisition Service seeks a contractor to provide software development and maintenance support for the System for Award Management (SAM.gov). This effort requires the contractor to work directly within the existing system environment, which has been under continuous development since 2012. The incoming contractor will execute a transition from incumbent operations currently held by IBM Federal, taking full responsibility for all active development sprints, defect backlogs, and release management activities. The legacy codebase includes multiple subsystems developed across several prior contract periods, resulting in heterogeneous technology stacks that the contractor must be prepared to support concurrently. GSA anticipates that the first six months of the contract will be heavily weighted toward stabilization and documentation of the existing system before new feature development proceeds at full velocity. The Government's Quality Assurance Surveillance Plan includes specific metrics for transition risk during this stabilization phase.

The Department of Homeland Security, Customs and Border Protection (CBP), solicits proposals for the sustainment and modernization of the Automated Commercial Environment (ACE), the primary trade processing system for all import and export activity at U.S. ports of entry. This acquisition requires the selected contractor to assume full responsibility for an existing system that processes more than $3 trillion in trade annually and interfaces with more than 48 Partner Government Agencies. The transition from the incumbent contractor must be executed without interrupting trade processing operations. The legacy codebase spans over 15 years of accumulated development and includes components written in COBOL, Java EE, and more recent cloud-native services. Offerors must submit a Transition Risk Management Plan as a separate volume of their proposal, describing in detail how they will manage knowledge transfer from the current team, maintain the existing system during transition, and begin prioritized modernization activities in the base period.

The Department of Education, Office of Federal Student Aid (FSA), announces a solicitation for continued development and operational support of the StudentAid.gov platform and its underlying data services. This contract represents continuing work on a platform that serves over 43 million borrowers and applicants annually. The selected contractor will receive a comprehensive handoff package from the incumbent contractor, including all source code repositories, infrastructure-as-code scripts, API documentation, and outstanding feature roadmap commitments. The incoming contractor must demonstrate the capacity to onboard into the existing system rapidly, with mission-critical services such as the Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) processing pipeline remaining fully operational throughout transition. FSA's evaluation methodology includes an oral presentation component during which offerors will be asked to describe specific technical challenges encountered when taking over legacy codebases and the resolution approaches applied. Key personnel proposed for this effort must be available to begin transition activities within two weeks of contract award.

The Department of Defense, Defense Logistics Agency (DLA), releases this solicitation for enterprise resource planning support services for the Enterprise Business System (EBS), DLA's primary supply chain management and financial management platform built on SAP. This effort involves the contractor taking over an existing system from the current support team and assuming responsibility for all Tier 2 and Tier 3 technical support, configuration management, and enhancement development activities. The existing system comprises a heavily customized SAP ECC 6.0 environment with over 900 custom ABAP objects developed and accumulated over more than a decade of prior contract performance. DLA requires offerors to demonstrate specific technical experience with transition from incumbent SAP support contracts, particularly where the incoming contractor was required to absorb undocumented customizations and reconstruct functional design documentation. The period of performance includes a mandatory 60-day parallel operations phase during which transition from the outgoing contractor will be completed under Government oversight.

The Department of Veterans Affairs, Veterans Health Administration, requires all contractor personnel to perform work exclusively at the VA Medical Center located in Houston, Texas (Michael E. DeBakey VA Medical Center, 2002 Holcombe Blvd). No off-site work is authorized under this contract. Contractor employees must badge in and out daily using the facility's access control system, and all deliverables must be produced using government-furnished equipment located on the premises. The nature of the work, which includes direct patient record management and clinical data entry support, requires constant physical presence within secured medical facility zones. Contractors are prohibited from removing any government data, documents, or equipment from the premises. All work hours shall be performed between 7:00 AM and 5:00 PM Central Time, Monday through Friday, excluding federal holidays. Supervisory oversight will be conducted by the Contracting Officer's Representative physically present at the Houston facility. Any request to perform duties from an alternate location will be denied and may constitute grounds for contract termination. Contractor shall provide a staffing plan confirming the availability of all named personnel to report to the Houston, TX facility for the full period of performance.

This acquisition is issued by the Naval Facilities Engineering Systems Command (NAVFAC) Southeast in support of maintenance and repair operations at Naval Station Mayport, Florida. All work under this contract shall be performed at Naval Station Mayport, 4933 Moosehead Ave, Mayport, FL 32228. Contractor personnel must obtain installation access credentials prior to commencement of work and must be present on the installation during all periods of active performance. The scope of work includes structural inspections, HVAC maintenance, plumbing repairs, and electrical systems testing across multiple buildings on the installation. Due to operational security requirements and the need for immediate supervisory coordination, no remote or off-installation work arrangements will be permitted at any time. All tools, equipment, and materials must be staged within the designated contractor lay-down area on the installation. Personnel requiring access to restricted pier areas must obtain additional vetting through the installation security office. Work schedules shall be coordinated with the Base Civil Engineer and all personnel must sign in at the Contractor Control Point each morning before proceeding to assigned work areas.

The General Services Administration, Public Buildings Service, hereby solicits proposals for facilities management support services at the Byron G. Rogers Federal Building, 1961 Stout Street, Denver, Colorado 80294. The contractor shall provide all services on-site at the Byron G. Rogers Federal Building. This includes, but is not limited to, janitorial services, building systems monitoring, visitor management, and physical security support. All contractor employees must maintain a continuous physical presence during assigned shifts within the building. The building operates on a 24-hour, 7-day-per-week schedule, and contractor coverage must meet this requirement accordingly. Personnel files and time and attendance records will be reviewed by the COR on a biweekly basis at the building management office. Contractors are expressly forbidden from performing any contract tasks from locations other than the specified federal building. Equipment used in performance of this contract shall remain on-site at all times unless written approval for removal is obtained from the Contracting Officer. Proposals must include a site-specific staffing plan demonstrating the offeror's capacity to maintain full on-site coverage throughout the entire base period and all option years.

The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Savannah District, is seeking qualified contractors to provide environmental remediation services at the Savannah District Hazardous Waste Site located at Fort Stewart, Georgia. All remediation activities shall be conducted at the designated project site on Fort Stewart, 1020 Liberator Drive, Hinesville, GA 31314. Contractor personnel, including all subcontractors and specialty technicians, must perform all work physically at the site. There is no provision for off-site data processing, laboratory coordination, or administrative functions to be conducted outside of the government-established project office trailer located at the remediation site. Soil sampling, groundwater monitoring, waste characterization, and reporting activities must all be carried out by personnel who are on-site and badged into the restricted work zone daily. The Army retains the right to conduct unannounced inspections to verify contractor presence and compliance with site safety plans. All field notes, chain-of-custody forms, and preliminary findings must be documented using paper logs maintained in the on-site project office. Digital submissions of final deliverables shall originate from the government network terminal provided within the on-site trailer.

The Department of Homeland Security, U.S. Customs and Border Protection, Office of Facilities and Asset Management, requires comprehensive building operations and maintenance services for the CBP Land Port of Entry at Laredo, Texas. All contractor work shall be performed at the World Trade Bridge Port of Entry, 6002 Milo Rodriguez Jr. Blvd, Laredo, TX 78045. The physical and operational security demands of this mission-critical federal facility require that all personnel engaged under this contract be present at the port of entry during all hours of contract performance. No work may be conducted from off-site locations. Personnel must complete CBP-specific security training and receive a facility access badge before performing any duties. The contractor shall maintain a dedicated on-site supervisor during all operational shifts. Maintenance logs, inspection records, and service reports shall be completed on-site and submitted to the COR through the government's facility management system, which is accessible only from terminals located within the port of entry. Contractors found to be conducting contract-related work from unauthorized locations will be subject to immediate removal from the program.

The National Aeronautics and Space Administration, Glenn Research Center, located in Cleveland, Ohio, requires support services for wind tunnel operations and test article preparation under this solicitation. All work under this contract shall be performed at NASA Glenn Research Center, 21000 Brookpark Road, Cleveland, OH 44135. The unique nature of the test infrastructure, including the Icing Research Tunnel, the Supersonic Wind Tunnel, and associated diagnostic equipment, mandates that all contractor technicians, engineers, and support staff perform their assigned duties exclusively on the Center's premises. Physical manipulation of test hardware, sensor calibration, data acquisition systems operation, and real-time monitoring of test conditions require continuous on-site presence. Remote access to NASA's restricted research networks is not authorized for contractor personnel under this effort. All deliverables, including test reports, technical drawings, and calibration records, must be generated using government-furnished workstations within the assigned laboratory spaces at Glenn Research Center. The contractor shall maintain a minimum staffing level as specified in the Performance Work Statement at the Glenn facility every scheduled workday throughout the period of performance.

The Federal Bureau of Prisons, Federal Correctional Complex Tucson, is soliciting for medical services support including physician, nursing, and mental health staffing at the FCC Tucson facility. All services shall be delivered exclusively on-site at Federal Correctional Complex Tucson, 8901 S Wilmot Rd, Tucson, AZ 85756. Given the inherent security requirements of a federal correctional environment, no contractor healthcare provider may perform any patient care, recordkeeping, or clinical consultation activities from a location outside the secured perimeter of the institution. Providers must report to the Health Services Unit at the start of each scheduled shift and must remain within the facility compound for the duration of their tour of duty. Telemedicine services are specifically excluded from this contract scope. Credentialing, scheduling, and supervision of all contractor medical personnel shall be conducted by the Health Services Administrator located at the FCC Tucson campus. Contractors must demonstrate their ability to staff all required positions with individuals who can physically report to the Tucson facility for every scheduled shift. Failure to maintain required on-site staffing levels may result in deductions under the contract's quality assurance provisions.

The U.S. Department of Agriculture, Forest Service, Intermountain Region, requires trail construction and restoration services within the Bridger-Teton National Forest, Wyoming. All work under this contract must be performed at designated project locations within the Bridger-Teton National Forest, as specified in the individual task orders issued by the Contracting Officer's Representative. Contractor crews must be physically present at each designated trail segment during all active work days. Field personnel may not perform any portion of this work from remote administrative locations; all ground disturbance, drainage installation, tread construction, and revegetation activities are inherently field-based and require continuous on-site presence. The Forest Service will conduct regular field inspections to verify contractor crew presence and compliance with the project's Best Management Practices. Daily field logs, photographic documentation, and resource protection monitoring forms must be completed by crew personnel while physically located at the work site. Contractors shall provide a base camp location within or immediately adjacent to the forest during multi-day project periods and shall not demobilize personnel from the work area without prior written authorization from the COR.

The Department of Energy, Savannah River Site, Office of Environmental Management, requires nuclear facility deactivation and decommissioning support services at the Savannah River Site in Aiken, South Carolina. All contractor work shall be conducted at the Savannah River Site, located in Aiken, SC 29808. The radiological, nuclear safety, and national security considerations associated with this facility categorically preclude the performance of any work activities from off-site locations. All contractor personnel must complete site-specific radiological worker training, obtain a DOE security clearance as applicable, and physically badge into the controlled area assigned to their scope of work each day. Operations including equipment decontamination, building characterization surveys, waste packaging, and safety documentation must be carried out by personnel who are physically present within the facility boundaries. DOE Facility Representatives will conduct daily walk-throughs to verify contractor presence and work progress. All controlled documents, radiological survey records, and waste manifests must be maintained in the on-site document control center located within the project's designated trailer complex at the Savannah River Site.

The Department of State, Bureau of Overseas Buildings Operations, requires construction management and quality assurance services for the New Embassy Compound project in Nairobi, Kenya. All contractor construction management, inspection, and quality assurance personnel shall perform their duties at the embassy construction site located in Nairobi, Kenya. On-site presence is mandatory for all individuals performing inspection, materials testing, contractor oversight, and project documentation functions. The State Department's security and diplomatic facility standards require that all construction oversight activities be conducted in person at the Nairobi project site to ensure immediate identification and resolution of construction deficiencies. Contractor staff must sign in at the Diplomatic Security-controlled site entry point at the commencement of each work day. All field inspection reports, non-conformance notices, and quality control records must be generated and signed at the project site. The contractor shall maintain a full-time resident project manager and a qualified inspector at the Nairobi site for the duration of the construction phase. Personnel rotations must be pre-approved by the Contracting Officer and shall not result in any gap in on-site coverage.

The contractor shall provide all personnel necessary to perform the scope of work described herein. All work under this contract shall be performed onsite at the Department of Agriculture's regional administrative offices located at 8030 Excelsior Drive, Madison, Wisconsin 53717. Remote work arrangements will not be authorized under any circumstances. Contractor employees must report to the Madison, WI facility no later than 7:30 AM on each scheduled workday. The Government will provide workspace, network access terminals, and badged entry to the facility. Contractors must comply with all Federal building security protocols, including identity verification and visitor management procedures enforced at the Madison location. Travel reimbursement to and from the Madison facility on a daily basis is not an allowable cost under this contract. The offeror shall demonstrate in their technical approach a plan for staffing qualified personnel within commutable distance of Madison, Wisconsin, or provide documentation of relocation plans for proposed key personnel prior to contract award. All deliverables, including reports, data analyses, and briefing materials, shall be completed and submitted from the Madison, WI performance site.

Section C – Performance Requirements: This solicitation is issued by the Veterans Benefits Administration seeking IT systems integration support. The place of performance is the VBA regional processing center situated in Madison, Wisconsin. All contractor personnel identified in the staffing plan must perform their duties onsite at the Madison, WI facility on a full-time basis, defined as no fewer than forty hours per week. The Government anticipates a twelve-month base period with two twelve-month option periods. Offerors must confirm in their proposal that proposed staff can report onsite to Madison, Wisconsin within thirty calendar days of contract award. Telecommuting or remote access from locations outside of Madison, WI is strictly prohibited without prior written approval from the Contracting Officer's Representative. The contractor shall maintain a local presence in Madison for the duration of the contract period of performance.

The National Weather Service, Central Region Headquarters, requires operational maintenance and calibration support for meteorological data collection equipment. The selected contractor must furnish technicians who will work onsite at the NWS facility located in Madison, Wisconsin. The place of performance for all labor categories under this contract is exclusively the Madison, WI government facility. No off-site work is permitted. Technicians shall be available onsite in Madison during standard operating hours of 6:00 AM through 6:00 PM, Monday through Friday, and shall be on-call for emergency maintenance response within a two-hour drive of the Madison, Wisconsin location. Offerors shall provide evidence that proposed technicians either reside within the Madison metropolitan area or will relocate prior to the contract start date. The Government will not reimburse lodging expenses for personnel who choose to commute from outside the greater Madison, WI region on a recurring basis.

STATEMENT OF WORK – SECTION 3: PLACE OF PERFORMANCE. All services rendered under this contract shall be performed at the U.S. Geological Survey offices located within Madison, Wisconsin. Contractor personnel are required to be physically present onsite at the Madison, WI work location during all core business hours established by the agency. The USGS Madison Science Center serves as the sole authorized place of performance. No distributed or hybrid work models will be considered for this effort. The contractor is responsible for ensuring that all key personnel maintain valid facility access credentials for the Madison location throughout the contract period. Personnel replacements must be approved in advance and must be available to report onsite in Madison, Wisconsin within fifteen business days of approval. The offeror's management approach shall address how continuity of onsite coverage in Madison will be maintained during periods of planned and unplanned personnel absence.

The Social Security Administration, Great Lakes Program Service Center, hereby solicits proposals for records management and document digitization services. This is a full-time onsite requirement; all contractor employees must perform work at the SSA facility in Madison, Wisconsin. The Madison, WI site contains sensitive personally identifiable information that may not be removed from the premises under any conditions. As a result, contractor personnel must complete all assigned tasks within the secure boundaries of the Madison facility. Proposals that include provisions for off-site processing or remote access to document systems will be deemed technically unacceptable. The Government estimates a workforce of ten to fifteen contractor full-time equivalents performing onsite in Madison for the base year. Offerors shall include a staffing plan that explicitly addresses local recruitment within the Madison, Wisconsin labor market and identifies any planned subcontracting arrangements with firms based in the Madison area.

PART I – CONTRACT SCHEDULE, Section B: This firm-fixed-price contract is established to provide administrative support and program analyst services to the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency Region 5 field office. The exclusive place of performance is EPA's Madison, Wisconsin office. Contractor staff shall report onsite to the Madison, WI location each business day unless specifically excused in writing by the designated Contracting Officer's Representative. The agency does not authorize telework for any labor category included in this contract. All government-furnished equipment is permanently stationed at the Madison facility and shall not be removed. Contractor personnel must obtain and maintain the requisite background investigation clearance level required by EPA for access to the Madison, Wisconsin site. The offeror shall certify in Section K representations that proposed personnel are available and willing to perform onsite work in Madison, Wisconsin for the full duration of the contract, including any exercised option periods.

The Department of Transportation's Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration issues this solicitation for compliance audit and enforcement support services. Performance shall occur onsite at FMCSA's Madison, Wisconsin district office. All audit personnel must be physically present at the Madison, WI office when conducting case reviews, preparing enforcement documents, and attending agency coordination meetings. FMCSA does not permit remote access to enforcement case management systems from locations outside of the Madison facility. The contractor shall maintain a minimum of four full-time audit specialists onsite in Madison at all times during the contract period of performance. Proposed personnel must provide documentation confirming their ability to commute to or reside in Madison, Wisconsin. The contractor's quality control plan shall describe procedures for monitoring onsite attendance and performance at the Madison location. Unexcused absences from the Madison, WI facility may constitute grounds for a cure notice under the terms of this contract.

SCOPE OF SERVICES: The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Rock Island District, requires civil engineering design and drafting support for water resource infrastructure projects. All design activities shall be conducted onsite at the Corps' project coordination facility in Madison, Wisconsin. Contractor engineers and CAD technicians must maintain full-time onsite presence at the Madison, WI facility to collaborate directly with Government project managers and review agencies. The Corps will furnish dedicated workstations, design software licenses, and secure file storage at the Madison location. Contractors are not authorized to transfer design files or project data to off-site locations. The offeror shall demonstrate in their technical approach how they will staff the Madison, Wisconsin worksite with licensed professional engineers holding credentials recognized in the State of Wisconsin. Proposed personnel must be prepared to commence onsite work in Madison no later than ten calendar days following contract award.

The Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, Midwest Division, solicits offers for healthcare program integrity analysis services. The place of performance is CMS's Madison, Wisconsin regional analytical center. Analysts are required to perform all data processing and investigative review activities onsite at the Madison, WI facility due to data use agreement restrictions and HIPAA security requirements governing protected health information. No contractor-owned devices may be used; all work must be performed on government-furnished systems within the secure environment of the Madison location. Contractors must ensure proposed analysts possess the appropriate privacy training certifications prior to beginning onsite work in Madison, Wisconsin. The Government will provide a dedicated secure work area within the Madison facility for up to eight contractor personnel. Offerors shall address in their technical approach their plan for continuous onsite staffing at Madison, WI throughout the base period and all option periods, including contingency staffing procedures.

PERFORMANCE WORK STATEMENT – SECTION 4: The Department of Education's Office of Inspector General requires forensic audit and investigative support services. The primary place of performance is the OIG field office located in Madison, Wisconsin. Selected contractor personnel must be onsite at the Madison, WI office whenever conducting investigative interviews, reviewing grant documentation, or preparing case files for prosecutorial referral. Given the law enforcement sensitive nature of materials handled in this contract, no work product may be generated or stored outside of the Madison facility. The contractor shall assign a Madison-based site lead who is physically present onsite in Madison, Wisconsin no fewer than four days per week. All other assigned investigators must be available for onsite deployment to the Madison, WI location within twenty-four hours of notification. Offerors must identify in their staffing plan the residential locations of proposed personnel relative to Madison and confirm onsite availability commitments.

The Department of Homeland Security anticipates award of a single Indefinite Delivery Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) contract to support cybersecurity operations at multiple field offices across the continental United States. The successful offeror shall provide no fewer than twelve (12) full-time equivalent personnel, including a Program Manager, Deputy Program Manager, four (4) Senior Cybersecurity Analysts, three (3) Network Engineers, and at least two (2) Vulnerability Assessment Specialists. All personnel must maintain active Secret clearances at time of contract award. The contractor shall ensure continuous coverage during core business hours (0700–1800 local time) and shall provide on-call support during off-hours for critical incident response. The Government anticipates that the breadth of work, spanning threat monitoring, penetration testing, compliance reporting, and stakeholder coordination, will necessitate the full complement of staff described herein throughout the base period and all option years. Offerors whose proposed staffing plans reflect fewer than twelve personnel will be deemed technically unacceptable and removed from further consideration. The offeror shall submit a staffing plan as Volume III of the technical proposal, clearly identifying each labor category, the individual proposed, and their respective roles and responsibilities in support of this requirement.

The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Baltimore District, is soliciting proposals for environmental remediation and site restoration services at a formerly used defense site located in the Mid-Atlantic region. Given the scale of contamination, the complexity of multi-media remediation (soil, groundwater, and sediment), and the requirement for concurrent work across three distinct operable units, the Government has determined that a minimum of fifteen (15) qualified personnel must be engaged on this contract at all times during active remediation phases. The required team shall include a licensed Professional Engineer serving as Project Manager, a Certified Industrial Hygienist, at minimum two (2) licensed Geologists, a Health and Safety Officer, four (4) field technicians, a quality control manager, and sufficient support staff to maintain documentation, community relations, and regulatory liaison functions. The period of performance is estimated at thirty-six (36) months with two (2) twelve-month option periods. Offerors shall demonstrate in their Management Plan that the proposed team possesses the depth of expertise and numerical sufficiency to execute overlapping work streams without degradation of quality or schedule. Failure to staff to the required minimum levels at any point during performance may constitute a material breach of contract.

The Department of Veterans Affairs, Office of Information and Technology, hereby issues this Request for Proposal for the modernization and ongoing sustainment of its legacy benefits processing systems. The scope of work encompasses system architecture redesign, application development, data migration, testing and quality assurance, training, and help desk support. Based on the complexity and mission-critical nature of this effort, the VA requires the contractor to provide a dedicated project team of no less than twenty (20) full-time personnel. The staffing complement shall include, at a minimum, a Program Manager, an Agile Release Train Engineer, five (5) software developers with demonstrated Java and cloud platform experience, three (3) database administrators, two (2) DevSecOps engineers, a cybersecurity SME, two (2) business analysts, a training lead, and requisite quality assurance personnel. All staff must be U.S. citizens and obtain a VA Suitability determination prior to commencing work. The offeror's staffing plan must demonstrate that all twenty positions will be filled within thirty (30) days of contract award, with acceptable résumés for each proposed individual submitted as part of the technical proposal. The Government reserves the right to reject proposed personnel who do not meet stated qualifications.

The National Aeronautics and Space Administration, Goddard Space Flight Center, is seeking a contractor to provide engineering, integration, and test support services for an upcoming Earth science mission. The nature of the work, which includes systems engineering, instrument calibration, software verification, environmental testing, launch support, and post-launch on-orbit operations, necessitates a large, multidisciplinary team. The contractor shall maintain a team of at minimum fourteen (14) engineers and scientists throughout the performance period, with additional personnel required during critical mission phases such as environmental test campaigns and launch operations. The core team shall include systems engineers, mechanical engineers, electrical engineers, software engineers, and integration and test specialists with demonstrated spaceflight heritage. The offeror shall provide a staffing matrix in the proposal that maps each of the fourteen required positions to an individual, identifies their role, qualifications, and availability by contract phase. The Government understands that mission phases will drive variable workload and reserves the right to request surge staffing above the fourteen-person baseline; however, the minimum staffing level shall not fall below fourteen individuals at any time during the base period of performance.

The General Services Administration, Public Buildings Service, announces this solicitation for facility operations and maintenance services for a portfolio of seventeen federally owned buildings located in the National Capital Region. The combined gross square footage of the portfolio exceeds 4.2 million square feet, encompassing office buildings, courthouses, and a federal laboratory campus. To ensure adequate coverage and rapid response to maintenance needs across this geographically dispersed portfolio, the contractor shall provide a minimum staffing complement of thirty (30) full-time personnel. Required positions include a Contract Manager, an Operations Manager, six (6) licensed journeyman electricians, four (4) HVAC mechanics holding EPA 608 Universal certification, three (3) plumbers, two (2) elevator mechanics, two (2) fire suppression system technicians, a locksmith, a roofing specialist, and sufficient general maintenance workers to support all preventive and corrective maintenance tasks. The contractor shall also provide administrative staff to support work order management and reporting. All personnel shall be physically located within the National Capital Region and available to respond to emergency calls within two (2) hours of notification. Staffing plans must demonstrate no gaps in coverage for any building within the portfolio.

The Department of Justice, Executive Office for Immigration Review, requires comprehensive information technology support services to sustain and enhance court management systems used by immigration judges across more than seventy (70) immigration courts nationwide. The contractor must deploy a standing team of not fewer than eighteen (18) IT professionals to execute the full scope of work, which includes help desk operations, application maintenance and development, network administration, data center management, cybersecurity monitoring, and business intelligence reporting. The required staffing configuration shall include a Program Manager with PMP certification, a Deputy Program Manager, a Help Desk Manager overseeing a staff of at least four (4) Tier I and Tier II support specialists, three (3) application developers, two (2) database administrators, a network engineer, a cybersecurity analyst, a data warehouse specialist, and a configuration management specialist. The contractor shall maintain this minimum staffing level throughout all base and option periods. Proposed personnel must successfully complete a DOJ background investigation prior to being granted access to DOJ networks and systems. The staffing plan, including résumés for all eighteen key and non-key personnel, shall be submitted with Volume II of the technical proposal.

The Department of Transportation, Federal Aviation Administration, is soliciting for acquisition support and program management assistance services for the Aviation Safety organization. The breadth of this requirement includes acquisition planning, cost estimating, source selection support, contract administration, program schedule management, risk management, independent verification and validation, and stakeholder communications. Due to the volume of active acquisitions and the complexity of ongoing program portfolios, the FAA has determined that a team of no fewer than eleven (11) full-time contractor personnel is required to execute this work satisfactorily. The required team shall include a Senior Program Manager with at least fifteen (15) years of federal acquisition experience, a Cost Estimator with ICEAA certification, two (2) Contract Specialists with FAC-C Level II or equivalent credentials, two (2) Program Analysts, a Scheduler proficient in Primavera P6, a Risk Manager, an IVV Lead, and two (2) junior analysts providing research and documentation support. The contractor shall assign named individuals to each position prior to contract award; substitution of key personnel during the first twelve (12) months of performance is prohibited without written Contracting Officer approval. Staffing levels at or above eleven personnel shall be maintained throughout the entire period of performance.

The Environmental Protection Agency, Office of Water, requires scientific, technical, and programmatic support services to advance water quality monitoring, data analysis, regulatory development, and outreach activities under the Clean Water Act and Safe Drinking Water Act programs. The Government has estimated the total level of effort at approximately 28,000 labor hours annually, distributed across multiple task areas requiring diverse scientific and regulatory expertise. To execute this level of effort and maintain continuity across concurrent task orders, the contractor shall maintain a dedicated team of no fewer than sixteen (16) full-time equivalent personnel. The team composition shall include environmental scientists, hydrologists, chemists, toxicologists, policy analysts, GIS specialists, technical writers, and program support staff with direct experience supporting EPA regulatory programs. The offeror's staffing plan must demonstrate that the sixteen-person team collectively possesses the competencies required to address all task areas identified in the Performance Work Statement. The Government will evaluate the staffing plan for both the depth of individual expertise and the overall team's capacity to absorb new task orders within existing resources. The contractor shall not reduce staffing below sixteen FTEs without prior written approval from the Contracting Officer's Representative and a corresponding contract modification.

The Department of Energy, National Nuclear Security Administration, is issuing this solicitation for nuclear security program support services at a classified facility in the Southeast United States. The scope includes physical security assessments, force-on-force exercise planning and execution, vulnerability analysis, security technology evaluation, safeguards and security training development, and regulatory compliance support. Given the classified nature of operations, the 24-hour operational tempo of the facility, and the technical complexity of nuclear security disciplines, the contractor must provide a minimum of thirteen (13) cleared personnel at all times. The required team includes a Program Director with a minimum of twenty (20) years of combined military and nuclear security experience, a Deputy Program Director, three (3) Physical Security Specialists, two (2) Force-on-Force Exercise Specialists, two (2) Safeguards and Security Analysts, a Security Technology Engineer, a Training Specialist, and two (2) Compliance Analysts. All personnel must possess active Q clearances at time of award. Offerors shall submit a staffing plan demonstrating that all thirteen positions are filled by named individuals meeting the qualification requirements set forth in Section J, Attachment 3. Personnel proposed for this effort may be subject to Government approval prior to assignment.

The Department of Health and Human Services, Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, requires contractor support for its Medicare Administrative Contractor oversight and audit readiness functions. The contractor shall provide program integrity, audit management, financial analysis, IT systems support, and stakeholder communications services to enable CMS to effectively oversee a portfolio of regional contractors administering Medicare Parts A and B. The anticipated scope of work, based on historical task order volume and complexity, requires the contractor to maintain a core team of no fewer than twenty-two (22) full-time personnel. Required positions include a Program Manager, two (2) Deputy Program Managers each responsible for distinct functional areas, five (5) Program Integrity Analysts, three (3) Financial Analysts with CPA credentials, four (4) IT Specialists supporting audit management systems, two (2) Communications Specialists, a Quality Assurance Manager, two (2) Data Analysts, and supporting administrative staff. The contractor shall provide monthly staffing reports to the Contracting Officer's Representative certifying that the twenty-two-person staffing minimum has been maintained and identifying any vacancies, planned departures, or substitutions. The Government reserves the right to assess deductions from invoices for any period in which the contractor fails to maintain the minimum staffing level.

The Department of Veterans Affairs, Veterans Health Administration, hereby solicits qualified contractors to provide comprehensive medical transcription services for the VA Medical Center located in Phoenix, Arizona. The period of performance for this contract shall commence on the date of award and continue through a duration not to exceed nine (9) months. No option periods are contemplated under this acquisition. Contractors must demonstrate the capability to mobilize a fully trained transcription workforce within ten (10) calendar days of contract award. All work shall be performed in accordance with applicable VA Handbook guidelines and HIPAA privacy regulations. Invoices shall be submitted monthly via the VA Financial Services Center. The Government anticipates award of a single Firm-Fixed-Price contract. Interested parties shall submit capability statements no later than the date specified in the associated solicitation notice on SAM.gov. The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code applicable to this requirement is 561410, with a small business size standard of $16.5 million in average annual receipts.

The U.S. Department of Transportation, Federal Highway Administration, Region 7, is issuing this Request for Proposals for traffic data collection and analysis services along designated Interstate corridors within the State of Kansas. The period of performance shall be six (6) months from the date of contract execution, with no provisions for renewal or extension under this solicitation. Given the short-term nature of this requirement, offerors are advised that no option years will be exercised. Contractors shall provide all necessary equipment, personnel, and software platforms to collect real-time and historical traffic volume data. Deliverables include monthly progress reports, a mid-term analysis summary, and a final comprehensive data report due no later than thirty (30) days prior to contract expiration. Proposals must include a detailed technical approach, staffing plan, and price schedule. Award will be made to the offeror representing the best value to the Government using a tradeoff between technical merit and price.

SOLICITATION NOTICE: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), Goddard Space Flight Center, Office of Procurement, is seeking a qualified contractor to support the development and delivery of prototype sensor calibration hardware for the Earth Science Division. The period of performance for this effort is limited to eight (8) months from date of award. This is a firm-fixed-price, short-term developmental contract and does not include option periods. The contractor shall deliver a minimum of three functional prototype units, all associated technical documentation, test reports, and a lessons-learned summary. All deliverables must be submitted to the NASA Contracting Officer's Representative no later than the final month of performance. Milestone reviews are scheduled at months two, five, and seven. Due to the compressed schedule, offerors must demonstrate immediate availability of key personnel including a Systems Engineer and Hardware Integration Lead. Proposals shall be evaluated on technical capability, schedule feasibility, and price.

The U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service (ARS), Pacific West Area, announces this competitive solicitation for janitorial and custodial services at its research facility located in Albany, California. The period of performance shall not exceed eleven (11) months, commencing upon contract award. No option periods are included in this acquisition. This short-duration contract is being issued in anticipation of a full and open competition to be conducted at a later date. The contractor shall provide all labor, equipment, cleaning supplies, and supervision necessary to maintain the facility in a clean, safe, and orderly condition. Services shall be performed five days per week during normal operating hours, with occasional weekend coverage as directed by the Contracting Officer's Representative. The contractor must comply with all applicable USDA safety regulations and federal building access requirements. Proposals are due by the closing date specified in the solicitation and must include a technical approach and fully burdened labor rate schedule.

DEPARTMENT OF HOMELAND SECURITY — U.S. IMMIGRATION AND CUSTOMS ENFORCEMENT — OFFICE OF ACQUISITION MANAGEMENT. This solicitation is issued for professional language interpretation and translation services in support of field operations conducted by Enforcement and Removal Operations (ERO) in the Atlanta Area of Responsibility. The period of performance is ten (10) months from the effective date of award, with no option periods. The Government anticipates that this short-term contract will bridge operations until a longer-term Blanket Purchase Agreement is established agency-wide. Contractors must maintain a roster of qualified interpreters fluent in Spanish, Portuguese, Haitian Creole, and Mandarin, available for telephonic and in-person interpretation on a twenty-four-hour, seven-day-per-week basis. Invoices shall be submitted biweekly. The contractor is responsible for all background investigation coordination in compliance with DHS security requirements. Proposals must demonstrate past performance on similar federal language services contracts, provide sample interpreter credentials, and submit a fixed hourly rate schedule by language category.

REQUEST FOR PROPOSALS — DEPARTMENT OF THE INTERIOR, BUREAU OF RECLAMATION, LOWER COLORADO REGION. The Bureau of Reclamation requires hydrological survey and water quality assessment services for a series of monitoring stations located along the Colorado River corridor in Nevada and Arizona. The period of performance for this contract is seven (7) months, beginning on or about the anticipated award date identified in this solicitation. This acquisition does not include any option periods or provisions for extension beyond the stated seven-month duration. The contractor shall conduct bi-weekly field assessments, collect water samples in accordance with EPA-approved protocols, and submit laboratory analysis results to the Contracting Officer's Representative within five (5) business days of collection. A final comprehensive technical report summarizing all findings, trend analyses, and recommendations shall be delivered no later than two weeks before contract expiration. The Government will evaluate proposals based on technical approach, key personnel qualifications, and price. Proposals are limited to fifty pages excluding resumes and certifications.

The Department of Justice, Federal Bureau of Prisons, Mid-Atlantic Regional Office, hereby solicits offers for the provision of mental health counseling and psychiatric evaluation services at a designated federal correctional institution in Cumberland, Maryland. The period of performance under this contract shall be nine (9) months from the date of award. There are no option years associated with this procurement. The contractor shall provide a minimum of two licensed psychiatrists and four licensed clinical social workers, each holding valid credentials in the State of Maryland or holding equivalent federal licensure. Services shall be provided on-site five days per week, with on-call availability for psychiatric emergencies. All contractor personnel must successfully complete Bureau of Prisons background investigations and security orientation prior to commencing work. Monthly performance reports are required and must be submitted to the COR no later than the fifth business day of each succeeding month. Award will be made to the responsible offeror whose proposal represents the best value.

SOLICITATION: DEPARTMENT OF ENERGY — OFFICE OF ENVIRONMENTAL MANAGEMENT — SAVANNAH RIVER OPERATIONS OFFICE. This Request for Proposals is issued for specialized radioactive waste characterization support services at the Savannah River Site in Aiken, South Carolina. The period of performance is limited to eight (8) months from date of contract award, with no option periods contemplated. This requirement is being procured as a short-duration task to support accelerated characterization activities required for near-term regulatory compliance milestones. The contractor must provide a qualified radiological engineer, a health physics technician, and appropriate monitoring equipment on-site within fifteen (15) calendar days of award. All work shall conform to applicable DOE Orders, Nuclear Regulatory Commission standards, and site-specific radiological control procedures. The contractor shall deliver a final waste characterization report summarizing sampling results, dose assessments, and waste classification recommendations no later than the penultimate month of performance. Proposals shall be evaluated using best value criteria with technical and management approach equally weighted against price.

The Department of the Navy, Naval Supply Systems Command Fleet Logistics Center, Pearl Harbor, Hawaii, announces this competitive small business set-aside solicitation for grounds maintenance and landscaping services at Naval Station Pearl Harbor. The base period of performance is six (6) months from date of award. No option periods are included. This short-term requirement is necessitated by an expiring contract and the need to maintain continuity of base operations while a long-term follow-on solicitation is being developed. The contractor shall furnish all labor, tools, equipment, and materials required to maintain lawns, ornamental plantings, athletic fields, and right-of-way areas in compliance with applicable Navy activity standards. Weekly work schedules must be coordinated and approved by the Contracting Officer's Representative. A performance assessment will be conducted at month three to evaluate contractor compliance with Quality Assurance Surveillance Plan metrics. Any identified deficiencies must be corrected within three business days of written notification. Interested offerors must be registered and active in SAM.gov.

GENERAL SERVICES ADMINISTRATION — PUBLIC BUILDINGS SERVICE — NATIONAL CAPITAL REGION. This solicitation is issued to procure comprehensive moving and relocation services for federal agency personnel and office equipment associated with a consolidation effort at multiple facilities in the Washington, D.C. metropolitan area. The period of performance shall be five (5) months from the date of award. This procurement does not include option periods, and the Government does not anticipate any need for extension given the defined scope of the relocation project. The contractor shall develop and submit a detailed move management plan within ten (10) days of award, coordinate all logistics with agency POCs, provide all necessary packing materials and specialized handling equipment, and complete all relocations in accordance with the approved schedule. All moves involving classified materials or sensitive equipment shall be conducted under escort and in strict compliance with applicable GSA and agency security protocols. Proposals must include a technical approach narrative, a staffing and resources plan, and a price schedule reflecting all anticipated labor, equipment, and materials costs.
